=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T14:42:00Z ===
INTENT: Initialize harness system for MMS project
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - TASK-000: Created directory structure (.harness/, _memory/, subagents/results/)
  - Created features.json with 27 tasks across 5 phases
  - Created progress.txt (this file)
  - TASK-001: Created hook block-forbidden-packages.sh (blocks npm/bun install of forbidden packages)
  - TASK-002: Created hook block-forbidden-imports.sh (blocks writing code with forbidden imports)
  - Updated CLAUDE.md with SubagentTask pattern (sections 6-9)
  - Updated .claude/settings.json with PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
NEXT:
  - TASK-010 through TASK-040: Execute PHASE 1 parallel extraction (10 subagents)
  - Each subagent saves results to ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
BLOCKERS: None

=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Key principles:
1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work

=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
Target: 60fps with 60k tasks

Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
- Context compression losing constraints
- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
- Executing without permission
- Trusting tests over visual verification
- Using stale training data

=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
- xstate (over-engineered)
- next (need SPA, not SSR)
- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
- react@18 (must use react@19)

=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
- react@19.x
- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
- @belt/tools@latest
- @react-three/fiber@9.x
- @react-three/drei@10.x
- three@0.170+
- vite@6.x

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:00:00Z ===
INTENT: Enhance hooks to prevent harness violations
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - Enhanced session-start.sh with lock file system
    - Creates .harness/.lock if uncommitted changes detected
    - Creates .harness/.lock if harness files missing
    - Blocks all edits until issues resolved
  - Created harness-guard.sh (universal blocker)
    - Runs before Bash commands
    - Blocks destructive commands if lock exists
    - Allows git commands to fix the lock
  - Created pre-write-guard.sh
    - Runs before Edit/Write operations
    - Blocks if lock exists
    - Warns if no task registered as in_progress
  - Created post-commit-unlock.sh
    - Runs after Bash commands
    - Removes lock after successful git commit
  - Updated block-forbidden-packages.sh
    - Now also checks for harness lock
    - Allows git and read-only commands when locked
  - Updated settings.json with all hook configurations

HOOKS NOW ACTIVE:
  SessionStart:
    - session-start.sh → Creates locks if issues detected
  PreToolUse:Bash:
    - block-forbidden-packages.sh → Blocks forbidden + locked
  PreToolUse:Edit:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PreToolUse:Write:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PostToolUse:Write:
    - block-forbidden-imports.sh → Blocks forbidden imports
  PostToolUse:Bash:
    - post-commit-unlock.sh → Removes lock after commit

LOCK FILE SYSTEM:
  - .harness/.lock created at session start if:
    1. Git has uncommitted changes (UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES)
    2. .harness/progress.txt missing (MISSING_PROGRESS)
    3. .harness/features.json missing (MISSING_FEATURES)
  - Lock BLOCKS all destructive operations
  - Lock removed after: git commit (if git clean)
  - Allowed when locked: git status/add/commit, read-only commands

NEXT: ✅ Completed in previous session

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:10:00Z ===
INTENT: Execute PHASE 1 - Parallel Knowledge Extraction (10 subagents)
STATUS: completed
TASKS:
  - TASK-010: STACK - Packages researched
  - TASK-011: STACK - Versions validated
  - TASK-012: STACK - Package hallucinations
  - TASK-020: ARCH - World metro systems (web search)
  - TASK-021: ARCH - RailNova research (web search)
  - TASK-022: ARCH - SignatureRail research (web search)
  - TASK-023: ARCH - LOD Layers approach
  - TASK-030: FAILURES - What went wrong
  - TASK-031: FAILURES - Forgotten constraints
  - TASK-040: WORKING - Code that worked
INPUT: /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/genesis/attempt6202/chats/conversations.jsonl (9304 lines)
OUTPUT: ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
BLOCKERS: None

COMPLETED RESULTS (246 total entries):
  - TASK-010: 51 entries - Packages (React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9, belt/tools approved)
  - TASK-011: 31 entries - Versions (122-124 FPS achieved, React 18+BlueprintJS worked, React 19+PatternFly ready)
  - TASK-012: 16 entries - Hallucinations (BlueprintJS→PatternFly, xstate→belt/tools, hook bypass)
  - TASK-020: 27 entries - Metro systems (IBM Maximo, Railnova, ZEDAS, Dubai Metro 85 technicians)
  - TASK-021: 17 entries - RailNova (Railfleet, Gantt charts, ML defect classification 96%)
  - TASK-022: 15 entries - SignatureRail (TrainPlan since 1994, Marey charts, what-if)
  - TASK-023: 31 entries - LOD layers (4 levels, Hermite smoothstep, RBush indexing, 120+ FPS)
  - TASK-030: 23 entries - Failures (blank screen, context loss, wrong packages, state sync)
  - TASK-031: 15 entries - Constraints (5x BlueprintJS violation, 6x visual verification ignored)
  - TASK-040: 20 entries - Working code (KPICard, ThemeContext, DeckCanvas, 3-layer Sandwich)

KEY INSIGHTS:
  1. React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9 is the target stack
  2. 122-124 FPS achieved in previous attempts (exceeds 60fps target)
  3. Main failures: blank screens, AI forgetting constraints, wrong packages
  4. Working patterns: 3-layer "Sandwich" architecture (Workers/Canvas/HUD)
  5. LOD system: 4 levels with Hermite smoothstep crossfade transitions

NEXT: TASK-050 (PHASE 2 - Merge findings from all PHASE 1 subagents)

input.content:
# MMS Extracted Knowledge - PHASE 1 Consolidation

**Generated:** 2026-01-06T15:21:00Z
**Source:** 10 subagent extraction tasks (246 entries total)
**Status:** TASK-050 - Merged findings from all PHASE 1 subagents

---

## 1. STACK DECISIONS (TASK-010: 51 entries)

### 1.1 APPROVED Packages

| Package | Version | Reason |
|---------|---------|--------|
| react | 19.x | Required for PatternFly 6 |
| @patternfly/react-core | 6.x | Enterprise UI components |
| @belt/tools | latest | State machine (replaces @zazcart/tools) |
| @react-three/fiber | 9.x | React 19 compatible |
| @react-three/drei | 10.x | R3F 9 compatible |
| three | 0.170+ | WebGL engine |
| vite | 6.x | Build tool (SPA, not SSR) |
| d3-array | latest | extent(), max() for array ops |
| d3-time | latest | timeYear, timeMonth, timeWeek, timeDay |
| d3-time-format | latest | timeFormat() for date formatting |
| d3-scale | latest | scaleLinear for Minimap |
| d3-interpolate | latest | interpolateRgb for colors |
| d3-scale-chromatic | latest | YlOrRd for heatmap |
| typescript | latest | Strict mode |
| bun | latest | Package manager and runtime |
| playwright | latest | Visual testing via MCP |

### 1.2 REJECTED/FORBIDDEN Packages

| Package | Why Forbidden |
|---------|---------------|
| @blueprintjs/* | AI kept hallucinating this instead of PatternFly |
| @zazcart/* | Renamed to @belt/tools |
| xstate | Over-engineered |
| next | Need SPA, not SSR |
| zustand/redux/mobx | Use @belt/tools state-machine |
| react@18 | Must use React 19 for PatternFly 6 |
| puppeteer | Use Playwright MCP instead |

### 1.3 DISCUSSED (Evaluate as needed)

- pixi.js 8.x - Previously used, replaced by R3F
- deck.gl - Found over-engineered for timeline
- recharts, mapbox-gl, leaflet - Optional
- rbush - Spatial indexing for 60k+ items
- immer, lodash, date-fns, lru-cache - Utilities

---

## 2. VERSION VALIDATION (TASK-011: 31 entries)

### 2.1 Validated Working Combinations

**OLD STACK (React 18):**
- React 18.3.1 + BlueprintJS 6.1.0 + R3F 8.17.10 + Drei 9.114.3
- Performance: **122-124 FPS** (exceeds 60fps target)
- Bundle: 356 KB gzipped (under 500KB target)

**NEW STACK (React 19) - TARGET:**
- React 19.2.3 + PatternFly 6.4.0 + @patternfly/react-icons 6.4.0
- Validated working in HQ project
- Vite 7.3.0 + TypeScript 5.9.3

### 2.2 Known Compatibility Issues

1. **r3f-perf + Three.js 0.170+**: Console spam from troika dependency (0.49.0 incompatible)
2. **React 18 vs 19**: BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18, PatternFly 6 prefers React 19
3. **Tauri CLI + rustc 1.87**: Requires --locked flag due to home@0.5.12

### 2.3 Upgrade Path

- BlueprintJS → PatternFly migration was DEFERRED for demo
- @zazcart/toolkit → @belt/tools: Same library, renamed (no API migration needed)
- R3F 8 → R3F 9: Required for React 19

---

## 3. HALLUCINATIONS (TASK-012: 16 entries)

### 3.1 Package Hallucinations

| AI Used | Should Be | Occurrences |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| @blueprintjs/core | @patternfly/react-core | 5+ times |
| xstate | @belt/tools state-machine | 4+ times |
| zustand | @belt/tools (useSyncExternalStore) | 4+ times |
| react@18 | react@19 | 3+ times |
| @mms/toolkit | @zazcart/toolkit (now @belt/tools) | Multiple |

### 3.2 API Hallucinations

- **XState-like API on @belt/tools**: AI used `interpret`, `createMachine with states` instead of `createSlice` pattern
- **Classes.FLEX_CONTAINER**: Does not exist in BlueprintJS - AI hallucinated
- **TextStyle.alpha in Pixi.js v8**: Does not exist - use lighter fill color instead
- **BP5 classes with BP6 components**: Caused dark mode to not apply (bp5-card vs bp6-card)

### 3.3 Critical Discovery: Hook Bypass

> "PreToolUse: matcher = Write|Edit -> NAO intercepta Bash!"

AI discovered hooks only blocked Write/Edit but NOT Bash commands, allowing `bun add @blueprintjs/core` to bypass protection.

**SOLUTION:** Updated hooks to also intercept Bash commands for package installation.

---

## 4. METRO MAINTENANCE SYSTEMS (TASK-020: 27 entries)

### 4.1 Key Software Systems

| Software | Description | Users |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| **IBM Maximo** | Industry standard EAM/CMMS | BART, LIRR, Washington DC, Singapore |
| **Railnova Railfleet** | ECM3 fleet management | DB Cargo, SNCF, Eurotunnel |
| **ZEDAS Asset** | Railway-specific CMMS | European railways |
| **Agility CMMS** | Cloud-based EAM | Keolis Amey Metrolink UK |
| **CARL Source** | Customizable CMMS | European metros |

### 4.2 Dubai Metro Specifics (Keolis MHI)

- **Contract:** 15 years (9 base + 6 renewable) with RTA
- **Fleet:** 128 trains
- **Team:** 85 technicians working 3 shifts 24/7
- **Maintenance Types:**
  - Light: Every 14 days or quarterly
  - Heavy/Overhaul: Based on mileage
  - Overhaul LV2: ~15 days per train (bogie, traction motors, doors, etc.)

### 4.3 Industry Approaches

1. **Time/Cyclical**: Interventions at regular intervals (hours/cycles/km)
2. **Condition-based**: Sensor monitoring (temp, vibration, pressure, oil)
3. **Predictive**: AI/ML forecasting (IBM Maximo uses this)

### 4.4 Common Challenges

- Maintenance and operations are mutually exclusive
- Limited resources vs urgent needs tradeoff
- Communication across vast networks
- Climate change impacts (extreme weather)

---

## 5. RAILNOVA RESEARCH (TASK-021: 17 entries)

### 5.1 Product Suite

1. **Railster**: Hardware edge device for telemetry
2. **Railgenius**: Real-time monitoring + predictive maintenance
3. **Railfleet**: Digital fleet + maintenance management

### 5.2 Railfleet Features (Most Relevant)

- **Real-time asset tracking** with interactive map + geofencing
- **Maintenance planning dashboard** with event states (unplanned/planned/overdue/closed)
- **Gantt chart visualization** for availability scheduling
- **Preventive maintenance** auto-scheduled from telematic counters
- **Campaign management** for coordinated modifications
- **ML defect classification** at 96% precision
- **Integration:** IBM Maximo, MySQL, .Net, Oracle, SAP

### 5.3 UI Approach

- Dark theme with fixed position filters
- Maps with railway focus (not Google Maps)
- Clear icons and consistent color scheme
- Visual rule builder (no coding required)
- User actions at center of workflow

---

## 6. SIGNATURERAIL RESEARCH (TASK-022: 15 entries)

### 6.1 TrainPlan (Flagship since 1994)

- Train scheduling + timetabling
- Long-term, short-term, very short-term planning
- Automatic conflict validation
- **"What if" scenario planning**
- Extensive calendaring
- Resource allocation + constraint management

### 6.2 UI Approach

- **Train graphs (Marey charts)**: Time-distance diagrams
- Powerful graphic and tabular displays
- Complete display of service schedule information
- Visual clarity for accurate results
- Bulk modification capability

### 6.3 On-the-Day Operations

- Visual dashboards for real-time reassignment
- Tracks: fuel distances, maintenance distances, crew hours, costs
- Contingency layers (pre-saved)
- Focus on situational awareness during disruption

### 6.4 Key Quote

> "It used to take my team around 10 minutes to identify conflicts... with TrainPlan it takes 10 seconds."

---

## 7. LOD LAYERS APPROACH (TASK-023: 31 entries)

### 7.1 Four LOD Levels

| Level | Zoom | View | Granularity |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| MACRO/Strategic | < 0.3x | Heatmap | Years (10-12 year overview) |
| MESO/Tactical | 0.3-1.0x | DNA Bars | Quarters/Months |
| OPERATIONAL | 1.0-3.0x | DNA Gantt | Weeks/Months |
| MICRO/Execution | > 3.0x | Task Cards | Days |

### 7.2 Visual Implementations

**Task Cards (MICRO):**
- Task names, status badges, date ranges

### 7.3 Transition System

- **Hermite smoothstep** for opacity crossfade
- **CROSSFADE_RANGE** for smooth blend zones
- Example: At 0.34x → Strategic 30%, Tactical 70%

```javascript
const smoothStep = (value, edge0, edge1) => {
  const t = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (value - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0)));
  return t * t * (3 - 2 * t);
};
```

### 7.4 Performance

### 10.1 Three-Layer Architecture ("Sandwich")

```
LAYER 1: THE BRAIN (Background/Workers)
├── Data processing + constraint logic
├── Track/labor availability in threads
└── Output: Constraint Deck

LAYER 2: THE CANVAS (WebGL / R3F or deck.gl)

├── Task bars, dependency connections, heatmaps

=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Key principles:
1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work

=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
Target: 60fps with 60k tasks

Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
- Context compression losing constraints
- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
- Executing without permission
- Trusting tests over visual verification
- Using stale training data

=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
- xstate (over-engineered)
- next (need SPA, not SSR)
- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
- react@18 (must use react@19)

=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
- react@19.x
- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
- @belt/tools@latest
- @react-three/fiber@9.x
- @react-three/drei@10.x
- three@0.170+
- vite@6.x

file.content:
# RFQ

*Extracted from RFQ.pdf*

---

## Page 1

 
RFQ Inputs 
1.​ Scope of Work 
Develop a solution that allows the maintenance and operations team to efficiently plan and schedule 
preventative maintenance activities for two distinct rolling stock fleets, consisting of 49 and 79 trains 
each (a total of 128 trains), while fulfilling operational requirements across the network. The system 
should offer an automated, data-driven, and real-time mileage management approach, considering 
different preventive maintenance activities, current resources, and operational needs. The requirements 
can be divided into three main categories.  
●​ Maintenance Planning 
●​ Operational Planning 
●​ Resource Planning  
Maintenance Planning: Key requirement to meet the maintenance planning, 
●​ Fetching daily mileage for each train from a specific location. 
●​ Predicting maintenance schedules and dates based on real-time mileage data. 
●​ Ability to adjust the maintenance schedule based on available resources (man-hours), train 
availability, and maintenance tracks. 
●​ The software should be capable of providing short-term (weekly, monthly) and long-term 
(yearly 1-10) plans based on the fixed parameters (man-hours and tracks). 
●​ Offer guidance and warnings on mileage underrun or overrun, as they can impact overall 
mileage management. 
●​ Availability to perform simulations on various planning and scheduling options. 
 
Operation Planning: Key requirement to fulfil the operational planning 
●​ Recommend the train numbers to be used daily based on the fitness status and mileage. 
●​ Recommend the run assignment (mileage and the network) for each train. 
●​ Provide warnings and recommendations to correct or adjust fleet mileage by comparing the 
predicted mileage with the actual mileage. 
●​ Provide an interface between the maintenance and operations teams to communicate on 
train fitness and mileage management.  
             Resource Planning: Key requirement to fulfil the Resource planning 
●​ The resource planning should consider the manpower, spare costs and tracks available, 
which are fixed parameters. 
●​ The maintenance planning should consider the fixed parameters and avoid overutilization or 
underutilization of the resources throughout the contract period (12 years) 
●​ The software should be able to provide the maintenance costing for each fleet based on the 
mileage and maintenance plan. 


## Page 2

●​ It should be able to provide different models or strategies in mileage management reflecting 
the changes in total maintenance costs in the short term and long term. 
2.​ Background 
●​
At present, no dedicated tools are available for mileage management. 
●​
The existing platform, IBM Maximo, primarily serves as an asset management tool and is 
not specifically for managing mileage. 
●​
Currently, mileage management is being managed manually through Excel. 
●​
Existing setup currently depends on raw mileage data that is stored and collected 
manually (folder driven mileage data), which requires validation and system integration. 
●​
Different fleet types and importantly Increasing fleet size necessitate a centralized, 
automated solution to ensure better scheduling and compliance. 
●​
Limited integration between maintenance and operations on mileage decisions. 
●​
Risk of non-compliance due to mileage underrun/overrun as it is performed manually. 
●​
Lack of predictive simulations to evaluate planning options. 
●​
Insufficient visibility of resources vs. actual utilization. 
●​
Manual handling of data from multiple sources resulting in delays and inefficiencies. 
 
3.​ Software’s core deliverables 
The Mileage Management shall be capable of integrating, processing, and utilizing inputs from 
maintenance, operations, and other required input, ensuring an automated, data-driven, and rule-based 
approach. 
●​
Daily Mileage Data 
●​
Maintenance Plans 
●​
Maintenance Resources: 
o​ Man-hours (fixed parameter). 
o​ Track allocation for maintenance activities. 
o​ Spare parts availability and baseline cost. 
●​
Fleet Identification: Train IDs and fleet classification (Kinki Sharyo / Alstom) 
●​
Fleet availability and Train Fitness Status 
●​
Mileage Balance Monitoring based on recommended run assignment. 
●​
Daily train deployment requirements across the network. 
●​
Adjustments between predicted vs. actual mileage utilization. 
●​
Maintenance budget forecasting 
 
4.​ Software input 
●​ Train IDs and fleet type (Kinki Sharyo and Alstom) 
●​ Daily Mileage – Live data 
●​ Daily train fitness status 
●​ Daily operation requirement and command for each train run IDs 
●​ Maintenance plans (e.g. PM, overhauls) 


## Page 3

●​ Track availability 
●​ Man-hours per maintenance 
●​ Maintenance baseline cost per maintenance – material cost, service cost 
●​ Man-hour cost 
●​ Base Depot for each fleet (Identification of the designated maintenance depot for each 
fleet) 
 
5.​ Software output 
●​ Centralised dashboard – This should provide one high level customised output and other 
comprises complete data, mileage status 
●​ Current maintenance status of all trains in terms of mileage 
●​ Train run assignments (Optimised allocation of trains for daily service based on fitness 
and mileage balance) 
●​ Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities (Predicted schedule of upcoming 
preventive maintenance and overhauls) 
●​ Maintenance compliance status (Real-time tracking of adherence to maintenance 
requirements based on run assignments) 
●​ Monthly and yearly cost compliance (Comparison of actual vs. planned maintenance 
costs on a monthly and yearly basis) 
●​ Standard and custom report generation (PDF, Excel) 
●​ Simulation report of different models on mileage management 
●​ Email notification of the generated reports to the stakeholders 
 
6.​ User Interface 
●​ Web-based application (preferred) accessible on desktops and mobile devices. 
●​ Capability to integrate with MMS (Maximo IBM) 
●​ Data security and compliance with IT standards. 
●​ Scalable architecture to accommodate additional fleets and extended functionalities. 
●​ Customizable alerts/notifications 
●​ Full accessibility as an admin. 
 
7.​ Implementation support 
●​ Project implementation methodology and timeline. 
●​ Case studies or references of similar implementations. 
●​ Supply, installation, and commissioning of the Mileage Control Management software. 
●​ Warranty and post-deployment support, including upgradation as per regular software 
updates and needs. 
●​ Migration of existing maintenance data into the system. 
●​ User training and knowledge transfer (Comprehensive training sessions for system users 
to ensure smooth adoption) 


## Page 4

●​ Documentation (Detailed technical and user manuals covering all functionalities) 
 
8.​ Case studies 
 Example:  Workload (Man-hour projection required vs available): 
 
a) Without adjustment: 
 
 
b) With adjustment 
 
 
9.​ Phase deployment 
 
●​ Phase 1 – with regards to complete requirements as outlines above, phase 1 should have 
implementation stage to cover the mileage management and run assignments. 
●​ Phase 2 – This phase where the costing part is included and required output is driven. 


## Page 5

●​ Phase 3 – Inclusion of room for the additional fleet into the system for which system 
retains the capabilities to do so. Competence Management part to be added to the 
system which can regulate the competency of the user. 
●​ Phase 3 – To be defined (Future scope)

1→# Business Requirements
     2→
     3→> Extracted and adapted from the original RFQ (Request for Quotation)
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## 1. Scope of Work
     8→
     9→Develop a solution that allows the maintenance and operations team to efficiently plan and schedule preventative maintenance activities for **two distinct rolling stock fleets** (49 + 79 = 128 trains) while fulfilling operational requirements across the network.
    10→
    11→The system should offer an **automated, data-driven, and real-time mileage management** approach, considering:
    12→- Different preventive maintenance activities
    13→- Current resources
    14→- Operational needs
    15→
    16→### Requirement Categories
    17→
    18→1. **Maintenance Planning**
    19→2. **Operational Planning**
    20→3. **Resource Planning**
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## 2. Maintenance Planning Requirements
    25→
    26→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
    27→|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
    28→| MP-01 | Fetch daily mileage for each train from specific location | High | ❌ Mock data |
    29→| MP-02 | Predict maintenance schedules based on real-time mileage | High | ⚠️ Static prediction |
    30→| MP-03 | Adjust schedule based on resources (man-hours), train availability, tracks | High | ❌ Not implemented |
    31→| MP-04 | Provide short-term plans (weekly, monthly) | High | ✅ Meso/Micro views |
    32→| MP-05 | Provide long-term plans (yearly, 1-10 years) | High | ✅ Macro view |
    33→| MP-06 | Warnings on mileage underrun/overrun | Medium | ⚠️ Visual indicators |
    34→| MP-07 | Perform simulations on planning options | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## 3. Operational Planning Requirements
    39→
    40→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
    41→|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
    42→| OP-01 | Recommend train numbers for daily use based on fitness and mileage | High | ❌ Not implemented |
    43→| OP-02 | Recommend run assignment (mileage and network) per train | High | ❌ Not implemented |
    44→| OP-03 | Warnings to correct/adjust fleet mileage (predicted vs actual) | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
    45→| OP-04 | Interface between maintenance and operations teams | Medium | ⚠️ Single view only |
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## 4. Resource Planning Requirements
    50→
    51→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
    52→|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
    53→| RP-01 | Consider manpower, spare costs, and available tracks | High | ❌ Not implemented |
    54→| RP-02 | Avoid overutilization/underutilization across contract (12 years) | High | ❌ Not implemented |
    55→| RP-03 | Provide maintenance costing per fleet based on mileage/plan | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
    56→| RP-04 | Different mileage management strategies with cost impact | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## 5. Background & Current State
    61→
    62→### Current Pain Points (from RFQ)
    63→
    64→- ❌ No dedicated tools for mileage management
    65→- ❌ IBM Maximo is asset management only, not mileage-specific
    66→- ❌ Mileage managed manually via Excel
    67→- ❌ Raw mileage data stored manually (folder-driven)
    68→- ❌ Limited integration between maintenance and operations
    69→- ❌ Risk of non-compliance due to manual mileage tracking
    70→- ❌ No predictive simulations
    71→- ❌ Insufficient resource visibility vs utilization
    72→- ❌ Manual handling causes delays and inefficiencies
    73→
    74→### Strategic Opportunity
    75→
    76→This demo addresses these pain points with a **"Single Pane of Glass"** visualization that:
    77→
    78→1. Unifies strategic, tactical, and operational views
    79→2. Provides semantic zoom (Google Earth-like UX)
    80→3. Shows conflicts and resource constraints visually
    81→4. Enables future What-If simulation capabilities
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## 6. Software Core Deliverables
    86→
    87→### Required Inputs
    88→
    89→| Input | Source | Demo Implementation |
    90→|-------|--------|---------------------|
    91→| Daily Mileage Data | Live system | Mock generator |
    92→| Maintenance Plans | Backend | Static data |
    93→| Man-hours (fixed parameter) | Configuration | Not implemented |
    94→| Track allocation | Configuration | Not implemented |
    95→| Spare parts availability | Backend | Not implemented |
    96→| Train IDs and fleet classification | Configuration | ✅ Implemented |
    97→| Fleet availability and fitness status | Live system | Not implemented |
    98→| Daily deployment requirements | Operations | Not implemented |
    99→
   100→### Required Outputs
   101→
   102→| Output | Description | Demo Implementation |
   103→|--------|-------------|---------------------|
   104→| Centralized dashboard | High-level overview | ✅ Canvas visualization |
   105→| Maintenance status (mileage) | All trains | ⚠️ Visual only |
   106→| Train run assignments | Optimized allocation | ❌ Not implemented |
   107→| PM/overhaul activities | Completed and upcoming | ✅ Task visualization |
   108→| Compliance status | Real-time tracking | ❌ Not implemented |
   109→| Cost compliance | Monthly/yearly comparison | ❌ Not implemented |
   110→| Report generation | PDF, Excel | ❌ Not implemented |
   111→| Simulation reports | Mileage management models | ❌ Not implemented |
   112→| Email notifications | Stakeholder alerts | ❌ Not implemented |
   113→
   114→---
   115→
   116→## 7. User Interface Requirements
   117→
   118→| Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
   119→|-------------|----------|-------------|
   120→| Web-based application | High | ✅ Astro/React |
   121→| Desktop accessible | High | ✅ Implemented |
   122→| Mobile accessible | Medium | ⚠️ Not optimized |
   123→| IBM Maximo integration capability | High | ❌ Not implemented |
   124→| Data security compliance | High | N/A (demo) |
   125→| Scalable architecture | High | ✅ Modular monorepo |
   126→| Customizable alerts | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
   127→| Admin full access | Medium | N/A (demo) |
   128→
   129→---
   130→
   131→## 8. Implementation Support Requirements
   132→
   133→| Requirement | Notes |
   134→|-------------|-------|
   135→| Project methodology and timeline | Agile recommended |
   136→| Case studies/references | See docs/RESEARCH.md |
   137→| Supply, installation, commissioning | Phase deployment |
   138→| Warranty and post-deployment support | SLA to be defined |
   139→| Data migration | From existing Excel/Maximo |
   140→| User training | Knowledge transfer sessions |
   141→| Documentation | Technical and user manuals |
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→## 9. Phase Deployment Plan
   146→
   147→### Phase 1 (Current Demo Scope)
   148→- ✅ Mileage visualization
   149→- ✅ Multi-level views (Macro/Meso/Micro)
   150→- ✅ Basic task/conflict display
   151→- ⚠️ Run assignment visualization (partial)
   152→
   153→### Phase 2 (Next)
   154→- [ ] Costing integration
   155→- [ ] Resource constraint enforcement
   156→- [ ] What-If simulation engine
   157→- [ ] Report generation
   158→
   159→### Phase 3 (Future)
   160→- [ ] Additional fleet support
   161→- [ ] Competence management
   162→- [ ] IBM Maximo integration
   163→- [ ] Real-time mileage data
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→## 10. Case Study Reference
   168→
   169→### Workload Projection Example (from RFQ)
   170→
   171→**Without adjustment:**
   172→- Peaks exceed capacity in certain months
   173→- Resource conflicts cause delays
   174→
   175→**With adjustment (Equalizing):**
   176→- Workload distributed evenly
   177→- No capacity breaches
   178→- Cost predictability improved
   179→
   180→See `docs/RESEARCH.md` for industry best practices on workload equalization (Tokyo Metro "Equalizing" approach).
   181→
   182→---
   183→
   184→## 11. Success Metrics
   185→
   186→| Metric | Target | Current Demo |
   187→|--------|--------|--------------|
   188→| Train availability | >99% | N/A (visualization only) |
   189→| Mileage compliance | >95% | N/A |
   190→| Resource utilization | 70-90% | N/A |
   191→| User adoption | 100% planners | N/A |
   192→| Response time | <100ms | ✅ 60fps target |
   193→
   194→---
   195→
   196→## 12. RFQ Alignment Summary
   197→
   198→| RFQ Section | Addressed By |
   199→|-------------|--------------|
   200→| Short-term planning (weekly/monthly) | Meso + Micro layers |
   201→| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | Macro layer (heatmap) |
   202→| Simulations | Future: Scenario mode |
   203→| Mileage warnings | Color-coded tasks, conflicts |
   204→| Resource visibility | Future: Constraint Deck |
   205→| Maintenance-Operations interface | Single canvas view |
   206→
   207→---

# Business Requirements

> Extracted and adapted from the original RFQ (Request for Quotation)

---

## 1. Scope of Work

Develop a solution that allows the maintenance and operations team to efficiently plan and schedule preventative maintenance activities for **two distinct rolling stock fleets** (49 + 79 = 128 trains) while fulfilling operational requirements across the network.

The system should offer an **automated, data-driven, and real-time mileage management** approach, considering:
- Different preventive maintenance activities
- Current resources
- Operational needs

### Requirement Categories

1. **Maintenance Planning**
2. **Operational Planning**
3. **Resource Planning**

---

## 2. Maintenance Planning Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
| MP-01 | Fetch daily mileage for each train from specific location | High | ❌ Mock data |
| MP-02 | Predict maintenance schedules based on real-time mileage | High | ⚠️ Static prediction |
| MP-03 | Adjust schedule based on resources (man-hours), train availability, tracks | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| MP-04 | Provide short-term plans (weekly, monthly) | High | ✅ Meso/Micro views |
| MP-05 | Provide long-term plans (yearly, 1-10 years) | High | ✅ Macro view |
| MP-06 | Warnings on mileage underrun/overrun | Medium | ⚠️ Visual indicators |
| MP-07 | Perform simulations on planning options | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |

---

## 3. Operational Planning Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
| OP-01 | Recommend train numbers for daily use based on fitness and mileage | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| OP-02 | Recommend run assignment (mileage and network) per train | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| OP-03 | Warnings to correct/adjust fleet mileage (predicted vs actual) | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
| OP-04 | Interface between maintenance and operations teams | Medium | ⚠️ Single view only |

---

## 4. Resource Planning Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
|----|-------------|----------|-------------|
| RP-01 | Consider manpower, spare costs, and available tracks | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| RP-02 | Avoid overutilization/underutilization across contract (12 years) | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| RP-03 | Provide maintenance costing per fleet based on mileage/plan | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
| RP-04 | Different mileage management strategies with cost impact | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |

---

## 5. Background & Current State

### Current Pain Points (from RFQ)

- ❌ No dedicated tools for mileage management
- ❌ IBM Maximo is asset management only, not mileage-specific
- ❌ Mileage managed manually via Excel
- ❌ Raw mileage data stored manually (folder-driven)
- ❌ Limited integration between maintenance and operations
- ❌ Risk of non-compliance due to manual mileage tracking
- ❌ No predictive simulations
- ❌ Insufficient resource visibility vs utilization
- ❌ Manual handling causes delays and inefficiencies

### Strategic Opportunity

This demo addresses these pain points with a **"Single Pane of Glass"** visualization that:

1. Unifies strategic, tactical, and operational views
2. Provides semantic zoom (Google Earth-like UX)
3. Shows conflicts and resource constraints visually
4. Enables future What-If simulation capabilities

---

## 6. Software Core Deliverables

### Required Inputs

| Input | Source | Demo Implementation |
|-------|--------|---------------------|
| Daily Mileage Data | Live system | Mock generator |
| Maintenance Plans | Backend | Static data |
| Man-hours (fixed parameter) | Configuration | Not implemented |
| Track allocation | Configuration | Not implemented |
| Spare parts availability | Backend | Not implemented |
| Train IDs and fleet classification | Configuration | ✅ Implemented |
| Fleet availability and fitness status | Live system | Not implemented |
| Daily deployment requirements | Operations | Not implemented |

### Required Outputs

| Output | Description | Demo Implementation |
|--------|-------------|---------------------|
| Centralized dashboard | High-level overview | ✅ Canvas visualization |
| Maintenance status (mileage) | All trains | ⚠️ Visual only |
| Train run assignments | Optimized allocation | ❌ Not implemented |
| PM/overhaul activities | Completed and upcoming | ✅ Task visualization |
| Compliance status | Real-time tracking | ❌ Not implemented |
| Cost compliance | Monthly/yearly comparison | ❌ Not implemented |
| Report generation | PDF, Excel | ❌ Not implemented |
| Simulation reports | Mileage management models | ❌ Not implemented |
| Email notifications | Stakeholder alerts | ❌ Not implemented |

---

## 7. User Interface Requirements

| Requirement | Priority | Demo Status |
|-------------|----------|-------------|
| Web-based application | High | ✅ Astro/React |
| Desktop accessible | High | ✅ Implemented |
| Mobile accessible | Medium | ⚠️ Not optimized |
| IBM Maximo integration capability | High | ❌ Not implemented |
| Data security compliance | High | N/A (demo) |
| Scalable architecture | High | ✅ Modular monorepo |
| Customizable alerts | Medium | ❌ Not implemented |
| Admin full access | Medium | N/A (demo) |

---

## 8. Implementation Support Requirements

| Requirement | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| Project methodology and timeline | Agile recommended |
| Case studies/references | See docs/RESEARCH.md |
| Supply, installation, commissioning | Phase deployment |
| Warranty and post-deployment support | SLA to be defined |
| Data migration | From existing Excel/Maximo |
| User training | Knowledge transfer sessions |
| Documentation | Technical and user manuals |

---

## 9. Phase Deployment Plan

### Phase 1 (Current Demo Scope)
- ✅ Mileage visualization
- ✅ Multi-level views (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- ✅ Basic task/conflict display
- ⚠️ Run assignment visualization (partial)

### Phase 2 (Next)
- [ ] Costing integration
- [ ] Resource constraint enforcement
- [ ] What-If simulation engine
- [ ] Report generation

### Phase 3 (Future)
- [ ] Additional fleet support
- [ ] Competence management
- [ ] IBM Maximo integration
- [ ] Real-time mileage data

---

## 10. Case Study Reference

### Workload Projection Example (from RFQ)

**Without adjustment:**
- Peaks exceed capacity in certain months
- Resource conflicts cause delays

**With adjustment (Equalizing):**
- Workload distributed evenly
- No capacity breaches
- Cost predictability improved

See `docs/RESEARCH.md` for industry best practices on workload equalization (Tokyo Metro "Equalizing" approach).

---

## 11. Success Metrics

| Metric | Target | Current Demo |
|--------|--------|--------------|
| Train availability | >99% | N/A (visualization only) |
| Mileage compliance | >95% | N/A |
| Resource utilization | 70-90% | N/A |
| User adoption | 100% planners | N/A |
| Response time | <100ms | ✅ 60fps target |

---

## 12. RFQ Alignment Summary

| RFQ Section | Addressed By |
|-------------|--------------|
| Short-term planning (weekly/monthly) | Meso + Micro layers |
| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | Macro layer (heatmap) |
| Simulations | Future: Scenario mode |
| Mileage warnings | Color-coded tasks, conflicts |
| Resource visibility | Future: Constraint Deck |
| Maintenance-Operations interface | Single canvas view |

---

*Source: Original RFQ document from Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI*

   129→
   130→---
   131→
   132→## 6. Component Layout
   133→
   134→### 6.1 Main Layout Structure
   135→
   136→```
   137→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   138→│                    NAVBAR (BlueprintJS)                      │
   139→│  [☰]  [Title]              [Zoom: 1.00x] [LOD: MESO] [Reset]│
   140→├────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   141→│            │  TIMELINE HEADER (adaptive granularity)        │
   142→│  SIDEBAR   ├────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   143→│ (Blueprint │                                                 │
   144→│  Card)     │              R3F CANVAS                        │
   145→│            │                                                │
   146→│  Train     │    [MacroLayer | MesoLayer | MicroLayer]       │
   147→│  IDs       │                                                │
   148→│  (Synced   │                          [+]  ← Zoom Controls  │
   149→│   with     │                          [-]  (ButtonGroup)    │
   150→│   panY)    │  [LOD Legend]                                  │
   151→└────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   152→```
   153→
   154→### 6.2 Component Responsibilities
   155→
   156→| Component | Location | BlueprintJS | Function |
   157→|-----------|----------|-------------|----------|
   158→| Navbar | Top | `<Navbar>` | Title, zoom indicator, LOD badge, reset button |
   159→| Sidebar | Left | `<Card>` | Train IDs, syncs with panY |
   160→| Timeline Header | Top of canvas | Custom | Date labels with adaptive granularity |
   161→| Canvas | Center | R3F `<Canvas>` | WebGL rendering of LOD layers |
   162→| Zoom Controls | Bottom-right | `<ButtonGroup>` | +/- buttons for zoom |
   163→| LOD Indicator | Navbar | `<Tag>` | Shows MACRO/MESO/MICRO with Intent color |
   164→| Instructions | Top-center | `<Toaster>` | Auto-hiding onboarding message |
   165→
   166→---
   167→
   168→## 7. Synchronization Requirements
   169→
   170→### 7.1 Virtual Scroll Sync
   171→
   172→The sidebar scroll MUST be mathematically locked to the canvas camera:
   173→
   174→```typescript
   175→// Constants
   176→const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // pixels per train row
   177→
   178→// Sidebar scroll → Camera Y
   179→const cameraY = -sidebarScrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN;
   180→
   181→// Camera Y → Sidebar scroll
   182→const sidebarScrollTop = -cameraY * PX_PER_TRAIN;
   183→```
   184→
   185→### 7.2 Time Scrubber Sync
   186→
   187→The footer minimap controls the camera X position:
   188→
   189→```typescript
   190→// Scrubber position → Camera X
   191→const cameraX = scrubberValue * totalTimeRange;
   192→
   193→// Camera X → Scrubber position
   194→const scrubberValue = cameraX / totalTimeRange;
   195→```
   196→
   197→---
   198→
   199→## 8. Future Features: Scenario Mode
   200→
   201→### 8.1 Baseline vs Scenario
   202→
   203→| Mode | Description | Data | Editing |
   204→|------|-------------|------|---------|
   205→| **BASELINE** | Production plan | Backend | Restricted |
   206→| **SCENARIO** | Experimental fork | Client-side | Free |
   207→
   208→### 8.2 Scenario UI Concept
   209→
   210→```

   212→│  Mode: [● BASELINE]  [○ SCENARIO]                           │
   213→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   214→│  Active Scenario: "August Strike Impact"           [✎] [×]  │
   215→│                                                              │
   216→│  Delta from Baseline:                                        │
   217→│   • OPEX 2026: +$2.3M (+4.2%)  ▲                           │
   218→│   • Overrun Risk: 12 trains    ▲                           │
   219→│                                                              │
   220→│  [Discard] [Compare] [Apply to Baseline]                    │
   221→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   222→```
   223→
   224→---
   225→
   226→## 9. Future Features: Constraint Deck
   227→
   228→A context-aware sidebar exposing editable parameters for real-time simulations:
   229→
   230→### 9.1 Strategic Level Controls
   231→- Global Budget Cap ($)
   232→- Total Fleet Size
   233→- Contract Duration
   234→
   235→### 9.2 Tactical Level Controls
   236→- Depot Availability
   237→- Shift Patterns
   238→- Holiday Calendar
   239→
   240→### 9.3 Operational Level Controls
   241→- Train Details (Last Service, Km Balance)
   242→- Route Distances
   243→- Track Availability
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## 10. Accessibility Requirements
   248→
   249→### 10.1 Current Implementation
   250→
   251→- ✅ Mouse navigation (drag to pan, scroll to zoom)
   252→- ✅ Button controls for zoom (+/- buttons)
   253→- ✅ Reset view button
   254→- ✅ Visual feedback (LOD indicator, zoom level)
   255→- ✅ Auto-hiding instructions tooltip
   256→
   257→### 10.2 Planned (see features.json F021-F022)
   258→
   259→- [ ] Tab navigation through tasks
   260→- [ ] Focus indicators on tasks
   261→- [ ] ARIA labels on interactive elements
   262→- [ ] Screen reader announcements for LOD changes
   263→
   264→### 10.3 Future Enhancements
   265→
   266→- [ ] High contrast mode
   267→- [ ] Reduced motion option
   268→- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (+/- for zoom, arrows for pan)
   269→
   270→---
   271→
   272→## 11. Performance Targets
   273→
   274→| Metric | Target | Rationale |
   275→|--------|--------|-----------|
   276→| Zoom/Pan Response | <16ms (60fps) | Zero perceived latency |
   277→| Initial Load | <3s | First meaningful paint |
   278→| LOD Transition | Seamless | No flickering |
   279→| Memory | Stable | No GC pauses |
   280→| Data Capacity | 60,000+ tasks | 10 years × 50 trains |
   281→
   282→---
   283→
   284→## 12. Demo Script Outline
   285→
   286→### Opening (30 seconds)
   287→> "This is a 12-year view of your entire maintenance program. Each color represents resource saturation."
   288→
   289→### Strategic Demo (2 minutes)
   290→> "Watch the heatmap show capacity patterns across years and fleets."
   291→
   292→### Zoom Transition (30 seconds)
   293→> "Now let's zoom in... Notice how the view transforms from heatmap to individual task blocks."
   294→
   295→### Tactical Demo (2 minutes)

   297→
   298→### Operational Demo (2 minutes)

   300→
   301→### Closing (30 seconds)

file.content:
# UX Specification: Fractal Canvas Maintenance Planner

## 1. Executive Vision

**Objective:** Replace disjointed spreadsheets and legacy ERP interfaces with a "Single Pane of Glass" solution for Rolling Stock Maintenance.

**Core Concept:** A continuous, zoomable timeline—like Google Maps but for time—that unifies Strategic Planning (12 Years), Tactical Balancing (Monthly), and Operational Execution (Daily).

**Key Differentiator:** The "Fractal" nature of data—strategic decisions instantly propagate to daily operations, and operational realities ripple up to strategic KPIs.

---

## 2. Core UX Principles

### 2.1 Interface Philosophy

- **Single Pane of Glass:** No navigation tabs or page reloads. Context changes solely via **Panning (Time)** and **Zooming (Granularity)**.

- **Semantic Zoom:** Data representation morphs based on zoom level:
  - *Zoomed out (12 years):* Heatmaps showing capacity saturation
  - *Mid zoom (months):* Task blocks showing resource loading
  - *Zoomed in (days):* Detailed cards showing individual trains & dependencies

- **Direct Manipulation:** Users drag and drop visual elements to reschedule (future feature).

### 2.2 The Google Earth Metaphor

Just as Google Earth allows seamless navigation from planet view to street level:

| Google Earth | MMS System |
|--------------|------------|
| Planet view | 12-year strategic heatmap |
| Country view | Annual/quarterly workload |
| City view | Monthly task distribution |
| Street view | Daily train schedules |

---

## 3. Zoom Level Hierarchy

### 3.1 Current Implementation (apps/canvas)

| Zoom Level | LOD | Time Scale | Visualization | Technical Layer |
|------------|-----|-----------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Macro** | < 0.5x | 10-20 years | Heatmap (density/health) | R3F InstancedMesh/Shader |
| **Meso** | 0.5-2.0x | 2-5 years | Task blocks (DNA pattern) | R3F InstancedMesh |
| **Micro** | > 2.0x | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards + grid | drei Html + CSS |

**Implementation:** `apps/canvas` - Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6.

**Tech Stack:**
- **Rendering:** React Three Fiber 8.x + drei 9.x
- **UI Components:** BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Navbar, Card, Button, Tag)
- **State:** useViewport hook for zoom, pan, LOD

### 3.2 Full Specification (Future)

| Temporal Scale | Visible Unit | Default Visualization | Analysis Focus |
|----------------|--------------|----------------------|----------------|
| **12 Years** | Years | Heatmap | Total capacity, CAPEX/OPEX |
| **3-5 Years** | Semesters | Stacked Bars | Fleet lifecycle patterns |
| **1-2 Years** | Quarters | Combo Chart | Seasonality, peaks |
| **Quadrimester** | Months | Workload Equalizer | Workload vs capacity |
| **1-3 Months** | Weeks | Weekly Bars | Resource conflicts |
| **2-4 Weeks** | Days (grouped) | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy |
| **1 Week** | Days | Detailed Gantt | Per-train status |
| **1-3 Days** | Hours | Capsules | Specific routes |

---

## 4. Visual Design Specifications

### 4.1 Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
|---------|-------|-----|-------|
| Background | Soft gray | `#f4f4f5` | zinc-100 |
| Tasks (normal) | Deep blue | `#1d4ed8` | blue-700 |
| Tasks (highlight) | Yellow | `#fbbf24` | amber-400 |
| Conflicts/Alerts | Danger red | `#ef4444` | red-500 |
| DNA pattern A | Orange | `#f59e0b` | amber-500 |
| DNA pattern B | Blue | `#3b82f6` | blue-500 |

### 4.2 Heatmap Gradient (MacroLayer)

```
Low occupancy    → High occupancy
Yellow (#fff3c4) → Orange (#fb923c) → Red (#dc2626)
```

### 4.3 Typography

- **Headers:** System font, semibold
- **Data labels:** Monospace for numbers
- **Task cards:** Sans-serif, readable at small sizes

---

## 5. Interaction Patterns

### 5.1 Navigation

| Action | Input | Result |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Pan horizontally | Drag / Arrow keys | Move through time |
| Pan vertically | Drag / Arrow keys | Move through trains |
| Zoom in | Scroll up / +key | More detail (LOD transition) |
| Zoom out | Scroll down / -key | Less detail (LOD transition) |

### 5.2 Selection (MicroLayer)

| Action | Input | Result |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Hover | Mouse over | Highlight task |
| Select | Click | Show details |
| Multi-select | Shift+Click | Select range (future) |
| Keyboard nav | Tab | Focus next task |
| Activate | Enter/Space | Select focused task |

### 5.3 Visual Feedback

| State | Visual Treatment |
|-------|------------------|
| Normal | Default colors |
| Hover | Brightness increase |
| Selected | Border highlight |
| Conflict | Red border/background |
| Disabled | Reduced opacity |

---

## 6. Component Layout

### 6.1 Main Layout Structure

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    NAVBAR (BlueprintJS)                      │
│  [☰]  [Title]              [Zoom: 1.00x] [LOD: MESO] [Reset]│
├────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│            │  TIMELINE HEADER (adaptive granularity)        │
│  SIDEBAR   ├────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ (Blueprint │                                                 │
│  Card)     │              R3F CANVAS                        │
│            │                                                │
│  Train     │    [MacroLayer | MesoLayer | MicroLayer]       │
│  IDs       │                                                │
│  (Synced   │                          [+]  ← Zoom Controls  │
│   with     │                          [-]  (ButtonGroup)    │
│   panY)    │  [LOD Legend]                                  │
└────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 6.2 Component Responsibilities

| Component | Location | BlueprintJS | Function |
|-----------|----------|-------------|----------|
| Navbar | Top | `<Navbar>` | Title, zoom indicator, LOD badge, reset button |
| Sidebar | Left | `<Card>` | Train IDs, syncs with panY |
| Timeline Header | Top of canvas | Custom | Date labels with adaptive granularity |
| Canvas | Center | R3F `<Canvas>` | WebGL rendering of LOD layers |
| Zoom Controls | Bottom-right | `<ButtonGroup>` | +/- buttons for zoom |
| LOD Indicator | Navbar | `<Tag>` | Shows MACRO/MESO/MICRO with Intent color |
| Instructions | Top-center | `<Toaster>` | Auto-hiding onboarding message |

---

## 7. Synchronization Requirements

### 7.1 Virtual Scroll Sync

The sidebar scroll MUST be mathematically locked to the canvas camera:

```typescript
// Constants
const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // pixels per train row

// Sidebar scroll → Camera Y
const cameraY = -sidebarScrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN;

// Camera Y → Sidebar scroll
const sidebarScrollTop = -cameraY * PX_PER_TRAIN;
```

### 7.2 Time Scrubber Sync

The footer minimap controls the camera X position:

```typescript
// Scrubber position → Camera X
const cameraX = scrubberValue * totalTimeRange;

// Camera X → Scrubber position
const scrubberValue = cameraX / totalTimeRange;
```

---

## 8. Future Features: Scenario Mode

### 8.1 Baseline vs Scenario

| Mode | Description | Data | Editing |
|------|-------------|------|---------|
| **BASELINE** | Production plan | Backend | Restricted |
| **SCENARIO** | Experimental fork | Client-side | Free |

### 8.2 Scenario UI Concept

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Mode: [● BASELINE]  [○ SCENARIO]                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Active Scenario: "August Strike Impact"           [✎] [×]  │
│                                                              │
│  Delta from Baseline:                                        │
│   • OPEX 2026: +$2.3M (+4.2%)  ▲                           │
│   • Overrun Risk: 12 trains    ▲                           │
│                                                              │
│  [Discard] [Compare] [Apply to Baseline]                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 9. Future Features: Constraint Deck

A context-aware sidebar exposing editable parameters for real-time simulations:

### 9.1 Strategic Level Controls
- Global Budget Cap ($)
- Total Fleet Size
- Contract Duration

### 9.2 Tactical Level Controls
- Depot Availability
- Shift Patterns
- Holiday Calendar

### 9.3 Operational Level Controls
- Train Details (Last Service, Km Balance)
- Route Distances
- Track Availability

---

## 10. Accessibility Requirements

### 10.1 Current Implementation

- ✅ Mouse navigation (drag to pan, scroll to zoom)
- ✅ Button controls for zoom (+/- buttons)
- ✅ Reset view button
- ✅ Visual feedback (LOD indicator, zoom level)
- ✅ Auto-hiding instructions tooltip

### 10.2 Planned (see features.json F021-F022)

- [ ] Tab navigation through tasks
- [ ] Focus indicators on tasks
- [ ] ARIA labels on interactive elements
- [ ] Screen reader announcements for LOD changes

### 10.3 Future Enhancements

- [ ] High contrast mode
- [ ] Reduced motion option
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (+/- for zoom, arrows for pan)

---

## 11. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Zoom/Pan Response | <16ms (60fps) | Zero perceived latency |
| Initial Load | <3s | First meaningful paint |
| LOD Transition | Seamless | No flickering |
| Memory | Stable | No GC pauses |
| Data Capacity | 60,000+ tasks | 10 years × 50 trains |

---

## 12. Demo Script Outline

### Opening (30 seconds)
> "This is a 12-year view of your entire maintenance program. Each color represents resource saturation."

### Strategic Demo (2 minutes)
> "Watch the heatmap show capacity patterns across years and fleets."

### Zoom Transition (30 seconds)
> "Now let's zoom in... Notice how the view transforms from heatmap to individual task blocks."

### Tactical Demo (2 minutes)
> "At this level, you see the DNA-like pattern of scheduled tasks across trains."

### Operational Demo (2 minutes)
> "Zooming to operational level... These are individual tasks with dependencies and conflict indicators."

### Closing (30 seconds)
> "This is the 'Single Pane of Glass'—from 12-year strategy to daily operations in one continuous view."

---

## 13. Design References

Visual specifications are in `design/`:

| Image | View | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| screen-1.png | Macro | Strategic heatmap overview |
| screen-2.png | Meso | DNA-like allocation strips |
| screen-3.jpg | Micro | Task cards with dependencies |
| screen-4.png | Meso | Dense diagonal pattern |
| screen-5.png | Meso | Color-coded resources |
| screen-6.png | Micro | Task details with conflicts |
| screen-7.png | UI | Header/sidebar layout |

---

*Adapted from UX_FOR_DEMO.md and PROJECT.md specifications*

# MMS Design Specification > **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Vite + React 18 + deck.gl > > **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only > > **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### 1.1 Objective Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. ### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points → Our Solutions | # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | |---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| | 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | | 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | | 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | | 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | | 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | | 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | | 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | | 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | | 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | --- ## 2. Design Philosophy ### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: | Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | |--------|--------------|-------------------| | **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | | **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | | **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | | **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | | **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | ### 2.2 Core Principles 1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks --- ## 3. Moodboard ### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. *Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* *Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* ### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. *Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* *GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* ### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. *DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* ### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software Fleet management patterns from railway industry. *Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* ### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. *Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* *Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* *SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* --- ## 4. Design System ### 4.1 Color Palette (Dark Mode) ``` BACKGROUND (Islands UI inspired) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ bg-canvas: #0A0A0A (Main canvas background) bg-panel: #141414 (Island panels) bg-elevated: #1C1C1C (Cards, popovers) bg-hover: #262626 (Interactive hover) bg-active: #2A2A2A (Active/selected) BORDERS (Subtle separation) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ border-subtle: #1F1F1F (Panel edges) border-default: #2E2E2E (Dividers) border-strong: #404040 (Focus rings) TEXT (High contrast) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ text-primary: #FAFAFA (Headlines) text-secondary: #A1A1A1 (Body) text-tertiary: #6B6B6B (Labels) text-disabled: #404040 (Disabled) SEMANTIC - MAINTENANCE TYPES (From RFQ) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ a-check: #10B981 (Green - Quick, 10k km) b-check: #3B82F6 (Blue - Standard, 50k km) c-check: #F59E0B (Amber - Major, 200k km) d-overhaul: #EF4444 (Red - Full, 800k km) SEMANTIC - STATUS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ success: #10B981 (Compliant) warning: #F59E0B (Approaching limit) danger: #EF4444 (Overrun/conflict) info: #3B82F6 (Informational) HEATMAP GRADIENT (Capacity/Workload) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ low: #FEF3C7 (Yellow-100) medium: #FB923C (Orange-400) high: #DC2626 (Red-600) critical: #7F1D1D (Red-900) SANDBOX MODE ACCENT (Distinct from baseline) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ sandbox-accent: #8B5CF6 (Violet-500) sandbox-bg: #0F0A1A (Violet-tinted canvas) sandbox-border: #6D28D9 (Violet-700) ``` ### 4.2 Typography ``` FONT FAMILY (Fleet-inspired) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Primary: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif Monospace: JetBrains Mono, monospace (IDs, numbers) SCALE ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ h1: 24px / 600 / -0.02em (Page titles) h2: 18px / 600 / -0.01em (Section headers) h3: 15px / 600 / 0 (Panel titles) body: 13px / 400 / 0 (Default text) caption: 11px / 500 / 0.02em (Labels) mono: 12px / 500 / 0 (Train IDs, dates) ``` ### 4.3 Spacing & Radius ``` SPACING (4px base unit) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ xs: 4px (Tight gaps) sm: 8px (Default gap) md: 12px (Panel padding) lg: 16px (Section gaps) xl: 24px (Major sections) 2xl: 32px (Page margins) RADIUS (Islands UI - subtle rounding) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ sm: 4px (Buttons, inputs) md: 6px (Cards, panels) lg: 8px (Modals) full: 9999px (Pills, badges) ``` --- ## 5. Application Shell ### 5.1 Layout Structure (Islands UI Pattern) ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ NAVBAR [48px] │ │ Logo │ [Planning] [Resources] [Reports] │ ◉ LIVE ○ SANDBOX │ User │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ ┌───────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ │ │ │ │ TIMELINE ISLAND [40px] ││ │ │ FLEET │ │ ← 2024 │ Jan │ Feb │ Mar │ Apr │ May │ Jun │ → → ││ │ │ ISLAND │ ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤│ │ │ │ │ ││ │ │ [160px] │ │ PLANNING CANVAS ││ │ │ │ │ (deck.gl) ││ │ │ Search... │ │ ││ │ │ ─────────── │ │ Semantic Zoom: MACRO → MESO → MICRO ││ │ │ ▼ Alstom(79) │ │ ││ │ │ ALT-001 ● │ │ ┌─────────┐││ │ │ ALT-002 ● │ │ │ MINIMAP │││ │ │ ALT-003 ◐ │ │ │ [120×60]│││ │ │ ... │ │ └─────────┘││ │ │ ▼ Kinki(49) │ │ ││ │ │ KNK-001 ● │ │ ││ │ │ │ │ ││ │ └───────────────┘ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ │ │ STATUS ISLAND [28px] ││ │ │ Zoom: 1.25x │ View: MESO │ Visible: 1,234 tasks │ Fleet: 128 trains ││ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### 5.2 Navbar Component ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [●] MMS Planning Resources Reports │ ◉ LIVE ○ SANDBOX │ 👤 Admin │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ↑ ↑ ↑ Navigation Mode Toggle User Menu (transparent tabs) (PRIMARY INTERACTION) ``` **Mode Toggle Behavior:** - `LIVE` → Production baseline (read-only interactions) - `SANDBOX` → Simulation mode (full editing enabled, violet accent) ### 5.3 Fleet Island (Sidebar) ``` ┌─────────────────┐ │ FLEET │ ← Panel title (h3) ├─────────────────┤ │ 🔍 Search... │ ← Filter input (transparent) │ │ │ ▼ Alstom (79) │ ← Collapsible group │ ALT-001 ● │ ← Train row: ID + status │ ALT-002 ● │ ● = operational │ ALT-003 ◐ │ ◐ = in maintenance │ ALT-004 ○ │ ○ = out of service │ ... │ │ │ │ ▼ Kinki (49) │ │ KNK-001 ● │ │ ... │ ├─────────────────┤ │ Shown: 128 │ ← Footer stats │ Selected: 0 │ └─────────────────┘ ``` **Row Height:** 24px (synced with canvas pxPerTrain) **Scroll:** Bidirectional sync with canvas Y position --- ## 6. Planning View (Main Canvas) ### 6.1 Semantic Zoom Levels The canvas morphs based on zoom level - "Google Earth for time." | Level | Zoom | Time Scale | Visualization | Purpose | |-------|------|-----------|---------------|---------| | **MACRO** | < 0.5x | 10-12 years | Heatmap cells | Strategic planning | | **MESO** | 0.5x - 2.0x | 1-3 years | Task blocks | Tactical planning | | **MICRO** | > 2.0x | 1-8 weeks | Detailed cards | Operational planning | ### 6.2 MACRO View (Strategic - zoom < 0.5x) ``` 2024 2025 2026 2027 2028 2029 2030 ... ├───────┼───────┼───────┼───────┼───────┼───────┼───────┼───── ALT-001│ ▓▓▒▒░ │ ▓▓▓▒▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▓▓▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▒▒░ │ ░░░░░ │ ALT-002│ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▒▒░ │ ▓▓▓▒▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▓▓▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▒▒░ │ ALT-003│ ▓▓▓▒▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▒▒░ │ ▓▓▓▒▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ▓▓▓▓▒ │ ▒▒░░░ │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ Legend: ░ Low workload ▒ Medium ▓ High █ Critical ``` **Implementation:** deck.gl HeatmapLayer or GridCellLayer **Cell Size:** Month × Train row **Color:** Yellow→Orange→Red gradient based on man-hour density **Interaction:** Click cell to zoom into that period ### 6.3 MESO View (Tactical - 0.5x to 2.0x) ``` Jan 2025 Feb 2025 Mar 2025 Apr 2025 ├────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────┼──────────── ALT-001│ ████████ │ ████ │ ██████████████│ ████ │ ALT-002│ ██████ │ ████████████ │ ████ │ ██████████ │ ALT-003│ ██████████│ ████ │ ████████ │ ████ │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ ... │ Legend: ████ = Task block (colored by type: A/B/C/D) ``` **Implementation:** deck.gl ScatterplotLayer or SolidPolygonLayer **Block Width:** Duration in days × pxPerDay **Block Height:** Fixed (20px) **Colors:** Maintenance type (A=green, B=blue, C=amber, D=red) **Interaction:** Hover for tooltip, click to select ### 6.4 MICRO View (Operational - zoom > 2.0x) ``` Mon 15 Tue 16 Wed 17 Thu 18 Fri 19 Sat 20 ├─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┤ ALT-001│ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ B-Check │──────┐ │ │ │ Track 2 │ 32 man-hrs │ 4 days │ │ │ │ └──────────────────────────────────┘ ▼ │ ALT-002│ ┌─────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Inspection │ │ │ │ Track 1 │ 8 hrs │ │ │ └─────────────────────┘ │ ALT-003│ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ C-Check ⚠️ CONFLICT│ │ │ │ Track 2 │ 160 man-hrs │ 6 days │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ``` **Implementation:** deck.gl TextLayer + IconLayer + PathLayer **Card Content:** Task type, track, man-hours, duration **Dependencies:** PathLayer with curved lines **Conflicts:** Red border + warning icon ### 6.5 LOD Transition Animation ``` Zoom: 0.1x ────── 0.5x ────── 2.0x ────── 5.0x │ │ │ LOD: MACRO → MESO → MICRO │ │ │ Crossfade: 0.4-0.5 1.8-2.0 (10% zone) (10% zone) ``` **Transition:** Opacity crossfade using Hermite smoothstep **Duration:** 200ms **Easing:** easeOutExpo --- ## 7. Sandbox Mode (PRIMARY FEATURE) ### 7.1 Concept Sandbox Mode enables **what-if scenario simulation** - the #1 missing feature in the RFQ. Component Specifications ### 8.1 Task Block (Meso/Micro) ```typescript interface TaskBlockProps { id: string; trainId: string; startDay: number; // Days from timeline start duration: number; // Days type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed'; hasConflict: boolean; isModified: boolean; // Sandbox: violet outline manHours: number; track: number; } ``` **Visual States:** | State | Treatment | |-------|-----------| | Default | Solid fill (type color) | | Hover | Brightness +15%, cursor pointer | | Selected | Cyan border (2px) | | Conflict | Red pulsing border | | Modified (Sandbox) | Violet dashed border | ### 8.2 KPI Card ```typescript interface KPICardProps { title: string; value: string | number; unit?: string; delta?: { value: number; direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'; label?: string; }; status?: 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger'; } ``` ``` ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ Fleet Availability │ ← title (text-tertiary, caption) │ │ │ 98.2% │ ← value (text-primary, h2) │ ▲ +0.3% vs last month │ ← delta (success/warning/danger) └──────────────────────────┘ ``` ### 8.3 Alert Banner ```typescript interface AlertBannerProps { type: 'warning' | 'danger' | 'info'; title: string; description?: string; action?: { label: string; onClick: () => void }; dismissible?: boolean; } ``` ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ⚠️ 3 trains approaching mileage overrun [View →] [×] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### 8.4 Minimap ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ░░░░▒▒▒▒▓▓▓░░░░▒▒▒▒░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ │ ░░▒▒▒░░░░░▒▒▓▓░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ │ ▒▒▒░░░░▒▒░░░░▓▓▒▒░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ │ ░░░░┌────┐░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ ← Viewport rectangle │ ░░░░│ │░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ │ ░░░░└────┘░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │ │ 2024 2030 2036 │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Size:** 120px × 60px **Position:** Bottom-right corner (fixed) **Interaction:** Click to navigate, drag viewport rectangle --- ## 9. Data Structures ### 9.1 Fleet Data (From RFQ) ```typescript // Fleet composition from RFQ const FLEET_CONFIG = { ALSTOM: { count: 79, prefix: 'ALT', depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' }, KINKI: { count: 49, prefix: 'KNK', depot: 'JEBEL_ALI' }, total: 128, }; // Contract period from RFQ const CONTRACT = { startYear: 2024, endYear: 2036, // 12 years }; // Maintenance intervals from RFQ const MAINTENANCE_TYPES = { 'A-Check': { intervalKm: 10_000, tolerance: 2_000, days: 1, manHours: 8 }, 'B-Check': { intervalKm: 50_000, tolerance: 5_000, days: 2, manHours: 32 }, 'C-Check': { intervalKm: 200_000, tolerance: 20_000, days: 3, manHours: 160 }, 'D-Overhaul': { intervalKm: 800_000, tolerance: 50_000, days: 20, manHours: 1200 }, }; ``` ### 9.2 Train Entity ```typescript interface Train { id: string; // "ALT-001" or "KNK-001" type: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI'; name: string; depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' | 'JEBEL_ALI'; currentMileageKm: number; avgDailyMileageKm: number; status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service'; lastMaintenanceDate: string; // ISO 8601 nextMaintenanceDue: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` ### 9.3 Task Entity ```typescript interface Task { id: string; trainId: string; startDate: string; // ISO 8601 endDate: string; // ISO 8601 type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled'; manHours: number; track: number; // 1-5 hasConflict: boolean; conflictReason?: string; dependencies?: string[]; // Task IDs estimatedCost: number; } ``` ### 9.4 Scenario Entity (Sandbox) ```typescript interface Scenario { id: string; name: string; description?: string; createdAt: string; modifiedAt: string; changes: ScenarioChange[]; metrics: { opexDelta: number; // vs baseline overrunRiskCount: number; trackUtilizationDelta: number; }; } interface ScenarioChange { type: 'move' | 'resize' | 'create' | 'delete' | 'modify'; taskId: string; before: Partial | null; after: Partial | null; timestamp: string; } ``` --- ## 10. Interactions & Gestures ### 10.1 Canvas Navigation | Input | Action | Handler | |-------|--------|---------| | Drag | Pan canvas | @use-gesture onDrag | | Scroll wheel | Zoom (cursor-centered) | @use-gesture onWheel | | Pinch (touch) | Zoom | @use-gesture onPinch | | Double-click | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | | Double-tap (touch) | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | ### 10.2 Task Interactions | Input | Live Mode | Sandbox Mode | |-------|-----------|--------------| | Hover | Tooltip | Tooltip + resize handles | | Click | Select task | Select task | | Drag task | - | Move (reschedule) | | Drag edge | - | Resize duration | | Right-click | View menu | Edit menu | | Delete key | - | Delete selected | ### 10.3 Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `+` / `=` | Zoom in | | `-` | Zoom out | | `0` | Reset zoom to 1x | | Arrow keys | Pan canvas | | `Escape` | Clear selection | | `Delete` | Delete task (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Z` | Undo (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Shift+Z` | Redo (Sandbox) | | `S` | Toggle Sandbox mode | --- ## 11. Performance Targets | Metric | Target | Notes | |--------|--------|-------| | FPS (idle) | ≥60 | All LOD levels | | FPS (interaction) | ≥30 | During pan/zoom | | Initial load | <3s | First meaningful paint | | Bundle size | <500KB | Gzipped | | Task count | 15,000+ | With viewport culling | | Viewport query | <5ms | RBush R-tree | | Memory | Stable | No leaks in 5min session | --- ## 12. Implementation Stack ``` FRAMEWORK ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Vite 5.x - Fast HMR, ESM-first build tool React 18.3.1 - UI components (required for BlueprintJS 6) TypeScript 5.x - Type safety VISUALIZATION ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ deck.gl 9.x - WebGL layers OrthographicView - 2D timeline coordinate system @use-gesture/react - Cross-platform input handling RBush - Spatial indexing (viewport culling) STATE MANAGEMENT ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ XState 5.x - Viewport & sandbox state machines Immer - Immutable scenario changes STYLING ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Tailwind CSS 3.x - Utility-first styling CSS Variables - Design tokens Inter font - Typography ``` --- ## 13. Phased Implementation Plan (Storybook) Following enterprise design best practices, implementation follows a **progressive refinement** approach using Storybook as the development environment. This ensures quality control at each stage before proceeding. ### 15.1 Overview ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ IMPLEMENTATION PHASES │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Phase 1 Phase 2 Phase 3 Phase 4 │ │ WIREFRAMES → LAYOUT → COMPONENTS → POLISH │ │ │ │ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ │ │ │ □ □ □ □ │ │ ■ ■ ■ ■ │ │ █ █ █ █ │ │ ▓ ▓ ▓ ▓ │ │ │ │ □ □ □ □ │ │ ■ ■ ■ ■ │ │ █ █ █ █ │ │ ▓ ▓ ▓ ▓ │ │ │ │ □ □ □ □ │ │ ■ ■ ■ ■ │ │ █ █ █ █ │ │ ▓ ▓ ▓ ▓ │ │ │ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ │ │ │ │ Low-fidelity Structural Interactive Production │ │ Placeholders Real layout Real data Animations │ │ No styling Design tokens Mock data Edge cases │ │ Dark theme Full interactivity Accessibility│ │ │ │ ● Approval ● Approval ● Approval ● Release │ │ Required Required Required Ready │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Critical Rule:** Each phase requires stakeholder approval before proceeding.
