# PLANO: Recomeçar MMS com Especificações Existentes

## Problema Identificado

O TASK-050 criou um "resumo de pesquisa" **inútil** (`_memory/extracted-knowledge.md`) que não serve para nenhuma equipe (UX, Dev, Produto).

**ESPECIFICAÇÕES COMPLETAS JÁ EXISTEM em:**
- `~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` (1500+ linhas - design system, componentes, Sandbox Mode)
- `~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` (semantic zoom, LOD, demo script)
- `~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` (RFQ mapeado)
- `~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/RFQ.md` (requisitos do cliente)

## Ação Corretiva

### 1. Deletar o lixo criado
- Remover `_memory/extracted-knowledge.md` (inútil)
- Resetar TASK-050 como `passes: false`

### 2. Recriar features.json baseado em DESIGN_SPEC.md

O DESIGN_SPEC.md já define 4 fases de implementação:

**Phase 1: WIREFRAMES**
- Application shell wireframe
- Fleet sidebar wireframe
- Planning canvas wireframe
- Timeline header wireframe
- Sandbox panel wireframe

**Phase 2: LAYOUT & DESIGN SYSTEM**
- Design tokens (cores, tipografia, espaçamento)
- Dark theme
- Islands UI layout

**Phase 3: INTERACTIVE COMPONENTS**
- UI primitives (Button, Card, Toggle, Badge)
- Data display (KPICard, AlertBanner, TrainRow, TaskBlock)
- Canvas layers (MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer)
- Sandbox components (Panel, Compare, Scenarios)

**Phase 4: INTEGRATION & POLISH**
- Full pages
- Animations
- Edge cases
- Accessibility

### 3. Single Source of Truth

Em vez de criar documentos novos, COPIAR os documentos existentes:

```
~/antonio/mms-last-attempt/_spec/
├── RFQ.md           → cp ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/RFQ.md
├── REQUIREMENTS.md  → cp ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/REQUIREMENTS.md
├── UX_SPEC.md       → cp ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md
├── DESIGN_SPEC.md   → cp ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md (truncar se necessário)
└── DOMAIN.md        → cp ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DOMAIN.md
```

### 4. Novo features.json

Mapear DIRETAMENTE do DESIGN_SPEC.md Section 15 (Storybook Implementation):

```json
{
  "phase1-wireframes": [
    "WF-001: ApplicationShell.stories.tsx",
    "WF-002: FleetSidebar.stories.tsx",
    "WF-003: PlanningCanvas.stories.tsx",
    "WF-004: TimelineHeader.stories.tsx",
    "WF-005: SandboxPanel.stories.tsx"
  ],
  "phase2-layout": [
    "LY-001: DesignTokens.stories.tsx",
    "LY-002: DarkTheme.stories.tsx",
    "LY-003: ApplicationShell styled",
    "LY-004: FleetIsland styled",
    "LY-005: TimelineIsland styled"
  ],
  "phase3-components": [
    "CP-001: Button/Card/Input/Toggle/Badge",
    "CP-002: KPICard/AlertBanner/TrainRow",
    "CP-003: TaskBlock (all states)",
    "CP-004: MacroLayer (heatmap)",
    "CP-005: MesoLayer (task blocks)",
    "CP-006: MicroLayer (detailed cards)",
    "CP-007: SandboxPanel/CompareView"
  ],
  "phase4-integration": [
    "INT-001: PlanningPage full",
    "INT-002: SandboxMode full",
    "INT-003: LOD transitions",
    "INT-004: Performance (15k tasks @ 60fps)"
  ]
}
```

## Arquivos Críticos a Usar

| Documento | Path | Propósito |
|-----------|------|-----------|
| RFQ | ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/RFQ.md | O que o cliente pediu |
| REQUIREMENTS | ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/REQUIREMENTS.md | Status de cada requisito |
| UX_SPEC | ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md | Semantic zoom, LOD |
| DESIGN_SPEC | ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md | Design system, componentes |
| DOMAIN | ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DOMAIN.md | Domínio do negócio |

## Perguntas para o Usuário

1. Devo deletar `_memory/extracted-knowledge.md` e começar do zero?
2. Devo copiar os docs existentes para `_spec/` ou apenas referenciar?
3. O stack deve ser React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9 (conforme CLAUDE.md) ou seguir o DESIGN_SPEC.md (React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + deck.gl)?
4. A prioridade é o **Sandbox Mode** (what-if scenarios) que é o diferencial do RFQ?

## Próximos Passos

1. Obter aprovação do usuário sobre este plano
2. Resetar harness (features.json, progress.txt)
3. Copiar documentos de spec
4. Criar features.json mapeado do DESIGN_SPEC
5. Começar implementação Phase 1 (Wireframes)

  - Created features.json with 27 tasks across 5 phases
  - Created progress.txt (this file)

  - Each subagent saves results to ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
BLOCKERS: None

=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Key principles:
1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work

=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
Target: 60fps with 60k tasks

Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
- Context compression losing constraints
- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
- Executing without permission
- Trusting tests over visual verification
- Using stale training data

=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
- xstate (over-engineered)
- next (need SPA, not SSR)
- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
- react@18 (must use react@19)

=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
- react@19.x
- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
- @belt/tools@latest
- @react-three/fiber@9.x
- @react-three/drei@10.x
- three@0.170+
- vite@6.x

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:00:00Z ===
INTENT: Enhance hooks to prevent harness violations
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - Enhanced session-start.sh with lock file system
    - Creates .harness/.lock if uncommitted changes detected
    - Creates .harness/.lock if harness files missing
    - Blocks all edits until issues resolved
  - Created harness-guard.sh (universal blocker)
    - Runs before Bash commands
    - Blocks destructive commands if lock exists
    - Allows git commands to fix the lock
  - Created pre-write-guard.sh
    - Runs before Edit/Write operations
    - Blocks if lock exists
    - Warns if no task registered as in_progress
  - Created post-commit-unlock.sh
    - Runs after Bash commands
    - Removes lock after successful git commit
  - Updated block-forbidden-packages.sh
    - Now also checks for harness lock
    - Allows git and read-only commands when locked
  - Updated settings.json with all hook configurations

HOOKS NOW ACTIVE:
  SessionStart:
    - session-start.sh → Creates locks if issues detected
  PreToolUse:Bash:
    - block-forbidden-packages.sh → Blocks forbidden + locked
  PreToolUse:Edit:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PreToolUse:Write:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PostToolUse:Write:
    - block-forbidden-imports.sh → Blocks forbidden imports
  PostToolUse:Bash:
    - post-commit-unlock.sh → Removes lock after commit

LOCK FILE SYSTEM:
  - .harness/.lock created at session start if:
    1. Git has uncommitted changes (UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES)
    2. .harness/progress.txt missing (MISSING_PROGRESS)
    3. .harness/features.json missing (MISSING_FEATURES)
  - Lock BLOCKS all destructive operations
  - Lock removed after: git commit (if git clean)
  - Allowed when locked: git status/add/commit, read-only commands

NEXT: ✅ Completed in previous session

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:10:00Z ===
INTENT: Execute PHASE 1 - Parallel Knowledge Extraction (10 subagents)
STATUS: completed
TASKS:
  - TASK-010: STACK - Packages researched
  - TASK-011: STACK - Versions validated

  - TASK-020: 27 entries - Metro systems (IBM Maximo, Railnova, ZEDAS, Dubai Metro 85 technicians)

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T14:42:00Z ===
INTENT: Initialize harness system for MMS project
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - TASK-000: Created directory structure (.harness/, _memory/, subagents/results/)
  - Created features.json with 27 tasks across 5 phases
  - Created progress.txt (this file)
  - TASK-001: Created hook block-forbidden-packages.sh (blocks npm/bun install of forbidden packages)
  - TASK-002: Created hook block-forbidden-imports.sh (blocks writing code with forbidden imports)
  - Updated CLAUDE.md with SubagentTask pattern (sections 6-9)
  - Updated .claude/settings.json with PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
NEXT:
  - TASK-010 through TASK-040: Execute PHASE 1 parallel extraction (10 subagents)
  - Each subagent saves results to ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
BLOCKERS: None

=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Key principles:
1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work

=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
Target: 60fps with 60k tasks

Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
- Context compression losing constraints
- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
- Executing without permission
- Trusting tests over visual verification
- Using stale training data

=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
- xstate (over-engineered)
- next (need SPA, not SSR)
- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
- react@18 (must use react@19)

=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
- react@19.x
- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
- @belt/tools@latest
- @react-three/fiber@9.x
- @react-three/drei@10.x
- three@0.170+
- vite@6.x

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:00:00Z ===
INTENT: Enhance hooks to prevent harness violations
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - Enhanced session-start.sh with lock file system
    - Creates .harness/.lock if uncommitted changes detected
    - Creates .harness/.lock if harness files missing
    - Blocks all edits until issues resolved
  - Created harness-guard.sh (universal blocker)
    - Runs before Bash commands
    - Blocks destructive commands if lock exists
    - Allows git commands to fix the lock
  - Created pre-write-guard.sh
    - Runs before Edit/Write operations
    - Blocks if lock exists
    - Warns if no task registered as in_progress
  - Created post-commit-unlock.sh
    - Runs after Bash commands
    - Removes lock after successful git commit
  - Updated block-forbidden-packages.sh
    - Now also checks for harness lock
    - Allows git and read-only commands when locked
  - Updated settings.json with all hook configurations

HOOKS NOW ACTIVE:
  SessionStart:
    - session-start.sh → Creates locks if issues detected
  PreToolUse:Bash:
    - block-forbidden-packages.sh → Blocks forbidden + locked
  PreToolUse:Edit:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PreToolUse:Write:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PostToolUse:Write:
    - block-forbidden-imports.sh → Blocks forbidden imports
  PostToolUse:Bash:
    - post-commit-unlock.sh → Removes lock after commit

LOCK FILE SYSTEM:
  - .harness/.lock created at session start if:
    1. Git has uncommitted changes (UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES)
    2. .harness/progress.txt missing (MISSING_PROGRESS)
    3. .harness/features.json missing (MISSING_FEATURES)
  - Lock BLOCKS all destructive operations
  - Lock removed after: git commit (if git clean)
  - Allowed when locked: git status/add/commit, read-only commands

NEXT: ✅ Completed in previous session

## CONTEXT
You are a subagent with NO MEMORY of previous sessions.

Extract ALL decisions about NPM PACKAGES that were researched or discussed in conversations.

## WHAT TO LOOK FOR
- NPM package names mentioned
- Specific versions discussed
- Reasons for choosing/rejecting packages
- Packages USER REQUESTED vs AI SUGGESTED

# SUBAGENT TASK: SPEC-001

## OBJECTIVE
Research PatternFly 6 design system via web search.

## WEB SEARCHES TO PERFORM
1. "PatternFly 6 React components 2025"
2. "PatternFly 6 design system documentation"
3. "PatternFly 6 dark mode theme"
4. "PatternFly 6 Page layout component"
5. "PatternFly 6 Masthead Sidebar Navigation"
6. "PatternFly 6 Card component"
7. "PatternFly 6 Table component"

## WHAT TO EXTRACT
- Available components list
- Layout components (Page, Masthead, Sidebar, PageSection)
- Data display components (Card, Table, Badge, Label)
- Form components
- Dark mode implementation
- Design tokens (colors, spacing)
- Integration with React 19

## OUTPUT FORMAT
Save to: ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-001.jsonl

Each line MUST be valid JSON:
{"task_id": "SPEC-001", "timestamp": "2026-01-06T15:40:00Z", "category": "layout|data|form|theme|token", "component_name": "...", "description": "...", "usage_example": "...", "source_url": "..."}

## IMPORTANT
- Use WebSearch tool to search
- Write results to the JSONL file using Write tool
- Focus on components useful for a scheduling/planning UI
- DO NOT execute any installation commands
- DO NOT create any React components yet
- ONLY research and document findings

content.text:
## Tarefa Concluida - SPEC-001: Pesquisa do PatternFly 6 Design System

Completei a pesquisa web sobre o PatternFly 6 e salvei todos os resultados em `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt/subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-001.jsonl`.

### Resumo das Descobertas

**Visao Geral:**
- PatternFly 6 versao 6.4.0 com 92 componentes
- Suporte completo a React 19 (Q2 2025) - nao-breaking, compativel com React 17/18
- Sistema de design tokens com 3 camadas (palette, base, semantic)

**Componentes de Layout:**
- `Page`, `PageSidebar`, `PageSection` - estrutura principal
- `Masthead` - cabecalho com navegacao e toolbar
- `Navigation` - vertical ou horizontal
- `Toolbar` - controles de filtro/acoes
- `Tabs` - navegacao entre conteudos

**Componentes de Dados:**
- `Card` - containers de conteudo (substituiu Tiles)
- `Table` - tabelas composiveis (@patternfly/react-table)
- `Badge`, `Label`, `Icon` - indicadores visuais
- `EmptyState`, `Progress`, `Spinner` - estados de carregamento

**Componentes de Formulario:**
- `Dropdown`, `Select`, `Menu`, `MenuToggle`
- `TextInput`, `TextInputGroup`, `Checkbox`
- `DatePicker`, `Form`, `Button`

**Tema e Tokens:**
- Dark mode via classe `pf-v6-theme-dark` no `<html>`
- Tokens CSS: `--pf-t--global--concept--property--modifier--state`
- Tokens React: `t_global_spacer_sm` em @patternfly/react-tokens
- Breakpoints em rem (nao pixels)

**Pacotes NPM:**
- `@patternfly/react-core@^6` - componentes principais
- `@patternfly/react-table@^6` - tabelas
- `@patternfly/react-icons` - icones
- `@patternfly/react-tokens` - tokens

**Para MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler):**
Recomendados: Page+Masthead+Sidebar para layout, Card para visualizacao de tarefas, Table para listas detalhadas, Toolbar para filtros, DatePicker para agendamento, Label/Badge para status.

Esse documento usa BlueprintJS (ERRADO) mas tem CONCEITOS VÁLIDOS.

## OBJECTIVE
Ler DESIGN_SPEC.md e extrair conceitos que podem ser adaptados para PatternFly 6.

## INPUT FILE
Read: /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md

## WHAT TO EXTRACT
1. Layout structure (Islands UI) → mapear para PatternFly Page/Masthead/Sidebar
2. Component specifications → mapear para PatternFly equivalentes
3. Interaction patterns → manter
4. Semantic zoom (MACRO/MESO/MICRO) → manter (não tem equivalente PatternFly, custom implementation)
5. Sandbox Mode (what-if) → manter conceito

## MAPPING RULES
- BlueprintJS Navbar → PatternFly Masthead
- BlueprintJS Card → PatternFly Card
- BlueprintJS Button → PatternFly Button
- BlueprintJS Tag → PatternFly Label/Badge
- BlueprintJS Toaster → PatternFly Alert/AlertGroup
- Custom colors → PatternFly design tokens (--pf-t--global--*)
- bp5-dark class → pf-v6-theme-dark class

## OUTPUT FORMAT
Save to: ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-002.jsonl

Each line MUST be valid JSON:
{"task_id": "SPEC-002", "concept": "Layout", "original_component": "BlueprintJS Navbar", "patternfly_equivalent": "Masthead", "adaptation_notes": "...", "source_section": "Section 5"}

content.text:
## Tarefa SPEC-002 Concluida

Extrai todos os conceitos do DESIGN_SPEC.md e mapeei para equivalentes PatternFly 6. O arquivo de resultados foi salvo em:

**Output:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt/subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-002.jsonl`

### Resumo da Extracao (46 conceitos mapeados):

#### Layout e Estrutura
- **Navbar** (BlueprintJS) → **Masthead** (PatternFly)
- **Islands UI** → **Page + PageSection + PageSidebar**
- **Fleet Island (160px sidebar)** → **PageSidebar + Nav**
- **Status Island (28px footer)** → **PageSection variant=footer**

#### Componentes UI
- **Button, Card, Tag** → Mapeamento direto para equivalentes PatternFly
- **Tooltip, Popover** → PatternFly Tooltip, Dropdown
- **Alert Banner** → PatternFly Alert/AlertGroup
- **KPI Card** → Card + DescriptionList
- **Search** → SearchInput
- **Collapsible Groups** → ExpandableSection ou Accordion

#### Tema e Tokens
- **bp5-dark** → **pf-v6-theme-dark**
- Tokens de cor customizados (bg-canvas #0A0A0A, etc.)
- Tokens de manutencao (A-Check verde, B-Check azul, etc.)
- Tokens de sandbox (violet accent #8B5CF6)

#### Conceitos Sem Equivalente PatternFly (implementacao custom)
- **Semantic Zoom (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)** - deck.gl/R3F layers
- **LOD Transitions** - animacao customizada
- **Minimap** - canvas customizado
- **Canvas interactions** - @use-gesture/react
- **Keyboard shortcuts** - event handlers customizados

#### Modo Sandbox
- **Mode Toggle** → PatternFly ToggleGroup
- **Sandbox Panel** → Card posicionado absolute
- **Compare View** → Grid + Cards ou Modal
- **Saved Scenarios** → DataList
agentId: ab1dc2d (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically:

1. **Session Start**: User triggered a session start with harness instructions, requesting to continue to the next task.

2. **Initial Action - TASK-050**: I read progress.txt and features.json, found TASK-050 was next (consolidate findings from PHASE 1 subagents). I read all 10 subagent results and created `_memory/extracted-knowledge.md` - a 400+ line document consolidating 246 entries.

3. **User Feedback #1 - CRITICAL**: User said the result was "péssimo" (terrible) - the knowledge document was useless:
   - Doesn't tell UX/Dev/Product teams WHAT to do
   - Ignored existing documentation in ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/
   - Wasted months of effort trusting Claude
   - The docs in ~/antonio/mms should be used as brainstorming sources

4. **Plan Mode Activated**: I went into plan mode to understand the scope. I discovered CRITICAL documentation:
   - ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/RFQ.md - Client requirements
   - ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/REQUIREMENTS.md - Requirements mapped with status
   - ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md - Semantic zoom, LOD levels
   - ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md - Complete design spec (1500+ lines)

5. **User Feedback #2 - CRITICAL**: User corrected me again:
   - I was ignoring CONSTRAINTS from CLAUDE.md (PatternFly 6, not BlueprintJS)
   - The existing docs use BlueprintJS (WRONG) but concepts are valid
   - Must use PatternFly 6 design system (no custom colors)
   - Must use subagents for web research
   - Must register everything in harness files
   - Context was almost ending and NOTHING was saved to disk!

6. **User Feedback #3 - CRITICAL**: "Use subagents!!!!!! registre as tasks para os subagents de modo que o resultado fique salvo em disco e registrado no harness"

7. **Correction Actions Taken**:
   - Updated progress.txt with session learnings
   - Reset features.json with new tasks (SPEC-001, SPEC-002, SPEC-003, SETUP-001, IMPL-001-007, PERF-001)
   - Deleted useless _memory/extracted-knowledge.md
   - Committed changes
   - Launched SPEC-001 subagent (PatternFly 6 research) - COMPLETED
   - Launched SPEC-002 subagent (Adapt DESIGN_SPEC to PatternFly) - COMPLETED

Key technical details:
- Stack: React 19 + PatternFly 6 + @belt/tools + R3F 9 + Drei 10 + Vite 6
- Forbidden: BlueprintJS, zustand, redux, mobx, xstate, Next.js, React 18
- PatternFly 6.4.0 has 92 components, supports React 19
- Dark mode: pf-v6-theme-dark class (not bp5-dark)
- Tokens: --pf-t--global--* pattern

Files modified:
- .harness/progress.txt - Added session learnings
- .harness/features.json - Reset to v2.0.0 with SPEC tasks
- _memory/extracted-knowledge.md - DELETED (was useless)
- ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-001.jsonl - PatternFly research output
- ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-002.jsonl - DESIGN_SPEC adaptation output

Pending: SPEC-003 (create executable spec), then implementation tasks

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is working on MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler) for Dubai Metro (Keolis MHI). The project has failed 5+ times due to AI forgetting constraints and creating useless outputs. The user explicitly requested:
   - Follow harness protocol (register tasks, commit, use subagents)
   - Use documentation in ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/ as BRAINSTORMING sources (not SSOT)
   - Strictly follow CLAUDE.md constraints (PatternFly 6, NOT BlueprintJS)
   - Use PatternFly design system (NO custom colors)
   - Create EXECUTABLE specs that UX/Dev/Product teams can actually use
   - Save everything to disk before context ends
   - Use SUBAGENTS for research tasks

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Required Stack**: React 19, @patternfly/react-core@6.x, @belt/tools, @react-three/fiber@9.x, @react-three/drei@10.x, three@0.170+, vite@6.x
   - **Forbidden Stack**: @blueprintjs/*, zustand, redux, mobx, xstate, next, react@18
   - **Semantic Zoom**: MACRO (heatmap), MESO (DNA blocks), MICRO (task cards)
   - **PatternFly 6.4.0**: 92 components, dark mode via `pf-v6-theme-dark` class, tokens: `--pf-t--global--*`
   - **Sandbox Mode**: What-if scenarios - #1 RFQ differentiator
   - **Islands UI Layout**: Page + Masthead + PageSidebar pattern
   - **Harness Protocol**: progress.txt, features.json, subagents/results/*.jsonl

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **/.harness/progress.txt** - Session log with learnings
     - Critical for context recovery between sessions
     - Added error analysis and next steps
     ```
     === SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:35:00Z ===
     INTENT: Correção de rumo após feedback do usuário
     STATUS: ERRO CRÍTICO - TASK-050 criou documento INÚTIL
     
     APRENDIZADOS DESTA SESSÃO:
     1. Docs em ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/ = BRAINSTORMING, não SSOT
     2. CONCEITOS VÁLIDOS dos docs existentes:
        - Semantic Zoom, LOD (MACRO/MESO/MICRO), Sandbox Mode, Islands UI
     3. STACK CORRETO (CLAUDE.md - NÃO NEGOCIÁVEL):
        - React 19 + PatternFly 6 + @belt/tools + R3F 9 + Drei 10 + Vite 6
     
     NEXT: SPEC-001 (Pesquisar PatternFly 6 via subagent)
     ```

   - **/.harness/features.json** - Task registry (reset to v2.0.0)
     - Contains all tasks with prompts for subagents
     - Key structure:
     ```json
     {
       "version": "2.0.0",
       "stack": {
         "required": ["react@19.x", "@patternfly/react-core@6.x", "@belt/tools@latest", "@react-three/fiber@9.x"],
         "forbidden": ["@blueprintjs/*", "zustand", "redux", "mobx", "xstate", "next", "react@18"]
       },
       "features": [
         {"id": "SPEC-001", "passes": true, "description": "PatternFly 6 research"},
         {"id": "SPEC-002", "passes": true (just completed), "description": "Adapt DESIGN_SPEC to PatternFly"},
         {"id": "SPEC-003", "passes": false, "description": "Create executable spec"},
         {"id": "SETUP-001", "passes": false, "description": "Create Vite + React 19 + PatternFly 6 project"},
         {"id": "IMPL-001" through "IMPL-007", "passes": false}
       ]
     }
     ```

   - **./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-001.jsonl** - PatternFly 6 research
     - Layout: Page, PageSidebar, PageSection, Masthead, Navigation
     - Data: Card, Table, Badge, Label
     - Theme: pf-v6-theme-dark, tokens --pf-t--global--*

   - **./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-002.jsonl** - DESIGN_SPEC adaptation
     - 46 concepts mapped from BlueprintJS to PatternFly
     - Key mappings: Navbar→Masthead, bp5-dark→pf-v6-theme-dark

   - **/_memory/extracted-knowledge.md** - DELETED (was useless)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **TASK-050 created useless document**:
     - Error: Created a "research summary" instead of executable spec
     - Fix: Deleted file, reset features.json, created SPEC-001/002/003 tasks
     - User feedback: "o resultado é lixo!!!" - teams can't use it

   - **Ignored CLAUDE.md constraints**:
     - Error: Proposed using existing specs that use BlueprintJS

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Bash - Block forbidden packages + Harness Guard
# Purpose: Prevent installation of forbidden packages AND enforce harness
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"

# Get the command being executed from stdin
COMMAND=$(cat)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HARNESS LOCK CHECK (allows git commands to fix the lock)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    # Allow git commands (needed to fix uncommitted changes)
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^git (status|add|commit|log|diff|stash|push|pull)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    # Allow read-only commands
    elif echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^(cat|less|head|tail|ls|tree|pwd|echo|which|type)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    else
        LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo ""
        echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
        echo "║ ⛔ COMMAND BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                           ║"
        echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
            echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
        fi
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Harness files missing                                 ║"
            echo "║ FIX: Create .harness/progress.txt and features.json           ║"
        fi
        echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
        echo ""
        exit 2  # BLOCK
    fi
fi

# List of forbidden packages (regex patterns)
FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "xstate"
    "zustand"
    "redux"
    "mobx"
    "next@"
    "next "
    "\"next\""
    "'next'"
)

# Check if command is npm/bun/yarn/pnpm install
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "(npm|bun|yarn|pnpm)\s+(add|install|i)\s+"; then
    for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qi "$pattern"; then
            echo ""
            echo "=========================================="
            echo "BLOCKED: Forbidden package detected!"
            echo "=========================================="
            echo ""
            echo "Pattern matched: $pattern"
            echo "Command: $COMMAND"
            echo ""
            echo "FORBIDDEN PACKAGES:"
            echo "  - @blueprintjs/* → use @patternfly/react-core"
            echo "  - @zazcart/* → doesn't exist"
            echo "  - xstate → over-engineered"
            echo "  - zustand/redux/mobx → use @belt/tools"
            echo "  - next → need SPA (use vite)"
            echo ""
            echo "See CLAUDE.md for required stack."
            echo "=========================================="
            exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
        fi
    done
fi

# Check for react@18 specifically
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "(npm|bun|yarn|pnpm)\s+(add|install|i)\s+.*react@18"; then
    echo ""
    echo "=========================================="
    echo "BLOCKED: react@18 is forbidden!"
    echo "=========================================="
    echo ""
    echo "Must use react@19 for PatternFly 6 compatibility."
    echo "Use: bun add react@19 react-dom@19"
    echo "=========================================="
    exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
fi

# Allow the command to proceed
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Edit/Write - Check harness compliance before edits
# Purpose: Ensure harness protocol is followed before any file edits
# Exit 2 to BLOCK, Exit 0 to allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"
PROGRESS_FILE=".harness/progress.txt"
FEATURES_FILE=".harness/features.json"

# Get the file being edited from environment (Claude Code sets this)
TARGET_FILE="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECK FOR LOCK FILE FIRST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)

    echo ""
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⛔ EDIT BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                              ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
        echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_PROGRESS"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/progress.txt not found                       ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_FEATURES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/features.json not found                      ║"
    fi

    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""

    exit 2  # BLOCK
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HARNESS FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.harness/|progress\.txt|features\.json"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow harness file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HOOK FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.claude/hooks/|\.claude/settings"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow hook file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FOR src/ EDITS - CHECK IF TASK IS REGISTERED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "^src/|/src/"; then
    # Check if any task is marked as in_progress (started_at set, passes: false)
    if [ -f "$FEATURES_FILE" ]; then
        # Look for a task that has started_at but not completed
        IN_PROGRESS=$(grep -E '"started_at":\s*"[^"]+.*"passes":\s*false' "$FEATURES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)

        if [ -z "$IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
            echo "║ ⚠️  WARNING: No task registered as in_progress                ║"
            echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
            echo "║ Before editing src/ files, you should:                        ║"
            echo "║   1. Find next task in features.json (passes: false)          ║"
            echo "║   2. Set started_at to current timestamp                      ║"
            echo "║   3. Then proceed with implementation                         ║"
            echo "║                                                               ║"
            echo "║ This ensures work is tracked across sessions.                 ║"
            echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
            echo ""
            # WARNING only, not blocking (would be too restrictive)
        fi
    fi
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# NO ISSUES - ALLOW EDIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Bash - Block forbidden packages + Harness Guard
# Purpose: Prevent installation of forbidden packages AND enforce harness
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"

# Get the command being executed from stdin
COMMAND=$(cat)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HARNESS LOCK CHECK (allows git commands to fix the lock)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    # Allow git commands (needed to fix uncommitted changes)
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^git (status|add|commit|log|diff|stash|push|pull)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    # Allow read-only commands
    elif echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^(cat|less|head|tail|ls|tree|pwd|echo|which|type)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    else
        LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo ""
        echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
        echo "║ ⛔ COMMAND BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                           ║"
        echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
            echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
        fi
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Harness files missing                                 ║"
            echo "║ FIX: Create .harness/progress.txt and features.json           ║"
        fi
        echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
        echo ""
        exit 2  # BLOCK
    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Bash - Block forbidden packages + Harness Guard
# Purpose: Prevent installation of forbidden packages AND enforce harness
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"

# Get the command being executed from stdin (with timeout to prevent hanging)
COMMAND=$(timeout 1 cat 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# If no command received, allow (fail-open for safety)
if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HARNESS LOCK CHECK (allows git commands to fix the lock)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    # Allow git commands (needed to fix uncommitted changes)
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^git |^git$"; then
        : # Allow git commands
    # Allow read-only commands
    elif echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^(cat|less|head|tail|ls|tree|pwd|echo|which|type|rm \.harness/\.lock)"; then
        : # Allow read-only + lock removal
    else
        LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo "" >&2
        echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" >&2
        echo "║ ⛔ COMMAND BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                           ║" >&2
        echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣" >&2
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║" >&2
            echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║" >&2
        fi
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Harness files missing                                 ║" >&2
            echo "║ FIX: Create .harness/progress.txt and features.json           ║" >&2
        fi
        echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" >&2
        echo "" >&2
        exit 2  # BLOCK
    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Bash - Block forbidden packages
# Exit 2 to BLOCK, Exit 0 to allow

# Read command from stdin (Claude Code passes it this way)
read -t 1 COMMAND || COMMAND=""

# If empty, try reading all of stdin
if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    COMMAND=$(cat 2>/dev/null || echo "")
fi

# Fail-open: if still no command, allow
if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK FORBIDDEN PACKAGES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "xstate"
    "zustand"
    "redux"
    "mobx"
)

# Check if command is npm/bun/yarn/pnpm install
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "(npm|bun|yarn|pnpm)\s+(add|install|i)\s+"; then
    for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qi "$pattern"; then
            echo "" >&2
            echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
            echo "⛔ BLOCKED: Forbidden package: $pattern" >&2
            echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
            echo "Use instead:" >&2
            echo "  @blueprintjs/* → @patternfly/react-core" >&2
            echo "  zustand/redux/mobx → @belt/tools" >&2
            echo "  xstate → simple state machine" >&2
            echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
            exit 2
        fi
    done

    # Block react@18
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "react@18"; then
        echo "" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        echo "⛔ BLOCKED: react@18 forbidden!" >&2
        echo "Must use react@19 for PatternFly 6" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        exit 2
    fi

    # Block next.js
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "\bnext\b|next@"; then
        echo "" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        echo "⛔ BLOCKED: Next.js forbidden!" >&2
        echo "Use Vite for SPA" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
fi

# Allow everything else
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Bash - Block forbidden packages + Harness Guard
# Purpose: Prevent installation of forbidden packages AND enforce harness
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"

# Get the command being executed from stdin
COMMAND=$(cat)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HARNESS LOCK CHECK (allows git commands to fix the lock)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    # Allow git commands (needed to fix uncommitted changes)
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^git (status|add|commit|log|diff|stash|push|pull)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    # Allow read-only commands
    elif echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "^(cat|less|head|tail|ls|tree|pwd|echo|which|type)"; then
        : # Allow - continue to other checks
    else
        LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo ""
        echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
        echo "║ ⛔ COMMAND BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                           ║"
        echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
            echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
        fi
        if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING"; then
            echo "║ REASON: Harness files missing                                 ║"
            echo "║ FIX: Create .harness/progress.txt and features.json           ║"
        fi
        echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
        echo ""
        exit 2  # BLOCK
    fi
fi

# List of forbidden packages (regex patterns)
FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "xstate"
    "zustand"
    "redux"
    "mobx"
    "next@"
    "next "
    "\"next\""
    "'next'"
)

# Check if command is npm/bun/yarn/pnpm install
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "(npm|bun|yarn|pnpm)\s+(add|install|i)\s+"; then
    for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qi "$pattern"; then
            echo ""
            echo "=========================================="
            echo "BLOCKED: Forbidden package detected!"
            echo "=========================================="
            echo ""
            echo "Pattern matched: $pattern"
            echo "Command: $COMMAND"
            echo ""
            echo "FORBIDDEN PACKAGES:"
            echo "  - @blueprintjs/* → use @patternfly/react-core"
            echo "  - @zazcart/* → doesn't exist"
            echo "  - xstate → over-engineered"
            echo "  - zustand/redux/mobx → use @belt/tools"
            echo "  - next → need SPA (use vite)"
            echo ""
            echo "See CLAUDE.md for required stack."
            echo "=========================================="
            exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
        fi
    done
fi

# Check for react@18 specifically
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "(npm|bun|yarn|pnpm)\s+(add|install|i)\s+.*react@18"; then
    echo ""
    echo "=========================================="
    echo "BLOCKED: react@18 is forbidden!"
    echo "=========================================="
    echo ""
    echo "Must use react@19 for PatternFly 6 compatibility."
    echo "Use: bun add react@19 react-dom@19"
    echo "=========================================="
    exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
fi

# Allow the command to proceed
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: SessionStart - Check harness state
# Exit 0 always (never block session start)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo ""
echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║ 🚀 MMS-LAST-ATTEMPT SESSION START                            ║"
echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Check harness files
PROGRESS=".harness/progress.txt"
FEATURES=".harness/features.json"

if [ -f "$PROGRESS" ]; then
    echo "✅ progress.txt exists"
else
    echo "⚠️  progress.txt MISSING - create it!"
fi

if [ -f "$FEATURES" ]; then
    echo "✅ features.json exists"
    # Show next pending task
    NEXT_TASK=$(grep -o '"id": "[^"]*"' "$FEATURES" | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
    echo "📋 Check features.json for next task"
else
    echo "⚠️  features.json MISSING - create it!"
fi

# Check git status
UNCOMMITTED=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$UNCOMMITTED" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "⚠️  UNCOMMITTED CHANGES: $UNCOMMITTED files"
    echo "   Run: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'"
fi

echo ""
echo "Remember: READ progress.txt → READ features.json → WORK"
echo ""

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Edit/Write - Check harness compliance before edits
# Purpose: Ensure harness protocol is followed before any file edits
# Exit 2 to BLOCK, Exit 0 to allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"
PROGRESS_FILE=".harness/progress.txt"
FEATURES_FILE=".harness/features.json"

# Get the file being edited from environment (Claude Code sets this)
TARGET_FILE="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECK FOR LOCK FILE FIRST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)

    echo ""
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⛔ EDIT BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                              ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
        echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_PROGRESS"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/progress.txt not found                       ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_FEATURES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/features.json not found                      ║"
    fi

    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""

    exit 2  # BLOCK
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HARNESS FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.harness/|progress\.txt|features\.json"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow harness file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HOOK FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.claude/hooks/|\.claude/settings"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow hook file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FOR src/ EDITS - CHECK IF TASK IS REGISTERED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "^src/|/src/"; then
    # Check if any task is marked as in_progress (started_at set, passes: false)
    if [ -f "$FEATURES_FILE" ]; then
        # Look for a task that has started_at but not completed
        IN_PROGRESS=$(grep -E '"started_at":\s*"[^"]+.*"passes":\s*false' "$FEATURES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)

        if [ -z "$IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
            echo "║ ⚠️  WARNING: No task registered as in_progress                ║"
            echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
            echo "║ Before editing src/ files, you should:                        ║"
            echo "║   1. Find next task in features.json (passes: false)          ║"
            echo "║   2. Set started_at to current timestamp                      ║"
            echo "║   3. Then proceed with implementation                         ║"
            echo "║                                                               ║"
            echo "║ This ensures work is tracked across sessions.                 ║"
            echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
            echo ""
            # WARNING only, not blocking (would be too restrictive)
        fi
    fi
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# NO ISSUES - ALLOW EDIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PostToolUse:Bash - Unlock after git commit
# Purpose: Remove lock file after successful git commit
# Exit 0 always (post hooks don't block)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"
TOOL_INPUT=$(cat)

# Check if this was a git commit command
if echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE "git commit"; then
    # Check if git is now clean
    GIT_STATUS=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)

    if [ -z "$GIT_STATUS" ]; then
        # Git is clean - remove lock if it was for uncommitted changes
        if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
            LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)

            if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
                # Remove the uncommitted changes line from lock
                grep -v "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES" "$LOCK_FILE" > "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null
                mv "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null

                # If lock file is now empty, remove it
                if [ ! -s "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
                    rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
                    echo ""
                    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
                    echo "║ ✅ HARNESS LOCK REMOVED                                       ║"
                    echo "║ Commit successful. You can now proceed with work.             ║"
                    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
                    echo ""
                fi
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Also check for progress.txt update
if echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE "progress\.txt"; then
    if [ -f ".harness/progress.txt" ] && [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
        # Remove MISSING_PROGRESS from lock
        grep -v "MISSING_PROGRESS" "$LOCK_FILE" > "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null
        mv "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null

        if [ ! -s "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
            rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PostToolUse:Write - Block forbidden imports in code
# Purpose: Prevent writing code with forbidden package imports
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

# Get the file content from stdin
CONTENT=$(cat)

# Get file path from environment (if available)
FILE_PATH="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-unknown}"

# Only check .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx files
if ! echo "$FILE_PATH" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then
    exit 0
fi

# List of forbidden import patterns
FORBIDDEN_IMPORTS=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "from ['\"]xstate"
    "from ['\"]zustand"
    "from ['\"]redux"
    "from ['\"]mobx"
    "from ['\"]next/"
    "from ['\"]next['\"]"
)

for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN_IMPORTS[@]}"; do
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
        echo ""
        echo "=========================================="
        echo "BLOCKED: Forbidden import detected!"
        echo "=========================================="
        echo ""
        echo "Pattern matched: $pattern"
        echo "File: $FILE_PATH"
        echo ""
        echo "CORRECT IMPORTS:"
        echo "  - @blueprintjs/* → @patternfly/react-core"
        echo "  - zustand/redux/mobx → @belt/tools"
        echo "  - xstate → @belt/tools state machine"
        echo "  - next → use vite instead"
        echo ""
        echo "See CLAUDE.md for required stack."
        echo "=========================================="
        exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
    fi
done

# Allow the write to proceed
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: SessionStart - Harness Enforcement + Lock System
# Purpose: Ensure harness protocol is followed, BLOCK if violated
# Exit 0 always (informational), but creates lock files that block other hooks

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

HARNESS_DIR=".harness"
LOCK_FILE="$HARNESS_DIR/.lock"
PROGRESS_FILE="$HARNESS_DIR/progress.txt"
FEATURES_FILE="$HARNESS_DIR/features.json"

# Clear any stale locks from previous sessions
rm -f "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🚀 MMS HARNESS - SESSION START"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo ""
echo "📅 Date: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
echo ""

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECK 1: UNCOMMITTED CHANGES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
GIT_STATUS=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$GIT_STATUS" ]; then
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⛔ CRITICAL: UNCOMMITTED CHANGES DETECTED                     ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
    echo "║ Previous session left uncommitted work!                       ║"
    echo "║                                                               ║"
    echo "║ MANDATORY ACTION:                                             ║"
    echo "║   1. Review: git status                                       ║"
    echo "║   2. Stage:  git add -A                                       ║"
    echo "║   3. Commit: git commit -m 'description'                      ║"
    echo "║                                                               ║"
    echo "║ ALL EDITS BLOCKED until committed!                            ║"
    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""
    echo "Uncommitted files:"
    echo "$GIT_STATUS" | head -10
    echo ""
    # Create lock file
    echo "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES" > "$LOCK_FILE"
    echo "Created lock: $LOCK_FILE (reason: uncommitted changes)"
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECK 2: HARNESS FILES EXIST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ ! -f "$PROGRESS_FILE" ]; then
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⚠️  WARNING: progress.txt NOT FOUND                           ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
    echo "║ Create .harness/progress.txt before starting work!            ║"
    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo "MISSING_PROGRESS" >> "$LOCK_FILE"
fi

if [ ! -f "$FEATURES_FILE" ]; then
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⚠️  WARNING: features.json NOT FOUND                          ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
    echo "║ Create .harness/features.json before starting work!           ║"
    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo "MISSING_FEATURES" >> "$LOCK_FILE"
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HARNESS PROTOCOL REMINDER
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    echo "✅ No blocking issues detected."
    echo ""
fi

echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ 📋 MANDATORY SESSION PROTOCOL                                   │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│                                                                 │"
echo "│  1. READ .harness/progress.txt    → What happened before?       │"
echo "│  2. READ .harness/features.json   → What task is next?          │"
echo "│  3. UPDATE features.json          → Register intent (started_at)│"
echo "│  4. EXECUTE one task              → Use subagents for complex   │"
echo "│  5. UPDATE features.json          → Mark complete (passes:true) │"
echo "│  6. UPDATE progress.txt           → Document what you did       │"
echo "│  7. COMMIT                        → git add -A && git commit    │"
echo "│                                                                 │"
echo "│  🔄 REPEAT for each task                                        │"
echo "│                                                                 │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ 🚫 FORBIDDEN PACKAGES (hooks will BLOCK installation)          │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ @blueprintjs/* → use @patternfly/react-core                     │"
echo "│ zustand/redux/mobx → use @belt/tools                            │"
echo "│ xstate → over-engineered                                        │"
echo "│ next → use vite (SPA, not SSR)                                  │"
echo "│ react@18 → must use react@19                                    │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ 🎯 SUBAGENT RULES                                               │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ • ALWAYS use subagents (preserves context)                      │"
echo "│ • ONE task = ONE subagent                                       │"
echo "│ • Subagent output → ./subagents/results/YYYYMMDD-TASK-XXX.jsonl │"
echo "│ • Each output line must include task_id as first field          │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

echo ""
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

exit 0

content.thinking:
Agora que li todos os arquivos de hooks, vou simplificar todos eles para serem mais robustos:

1. session-start.sh - Simplificar, não criar lock (apenas informativo)
2. pre-write-guard.sh - Simplificar, não usar lock
3. block-forbidden-imports.sh - Adicionar output para stderr
4. post-commit-unlock.sh - Não é mais necessário se não usamos lock

A estratégia vai ser:
- Remover o sistema de lock (muito complexo e bugado)
- Manter apenas os bloqueios essenciais (forbidden packages/imports)
- Session start apenas informativo

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse:Edit/Write - Check harness compliance before edits
# Purpose: Ensure harness protocol is followed before any file edits
# Exit 2 to BLOCK, Exit 0 to allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"
PROGRESS_FILE=".harness/progress.txt"
FEATURES_FILE=".harness/features.json"

# Get the file being edited from environment (Claude Code sets this)
TARGET_FILE="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECK FOR LOCK FILE FIRST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)

    echo ""
    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║ ⛔ EDIT BLOCKED BY HARNESS GUARD                              ║"
    echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: Uncommitted changes from previous session             ║"
        echo "║ FIX: git add -A && git commit -m 'message'                    ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_PROGRESS"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/progress.txt not found                       ║"
    fi

    if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "MISSING_FEATURES"; then
        echo "║ REASON: .harness/features.json not found                      ║"
    fi

    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""

    exit 2  # BLOCK
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HARNESS FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.harness/|progress\.txt|features\.json"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow harness file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALLOW EDITS TO HOOK FILES (always)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "\.claude/hooks/|\.claude/settings"; then
    exit 0  # Always allow hook file edits
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FOR src/ EDITS - CHECK IF TASK IS REGISTERED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if echo "$TARGET_FILE" | grep -qE "^src/|/src/"; then
    # Check if any task is marked as in_progress (started_at set, passes: false)
    if [ -f "$FEATURES_FILE" ]; then
        # Look for a task that has started_at but not completed
        IN_PROGRESS=$(grep -E '"started_at":\s*"[^"]+.*"passes":\s*false' "$FEATURES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)

        if [ -z "$IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
            echo "║ ⚠️  WARNING: No task registered as in_progress                ║"
            echo "╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
            echo "║ Before editing src/ files, you should:                        ║"
            echo "║   1. Find next task in features.json (passes: false)          ║"
            echo "║   2. Set started_at to current timestamp                      ║"
            echo "║   3. Then proceed with implementation                         ║"
            echo "║                                                               ║"
            echo "║ This ensures work is tracked across sessions.                 ║"
            echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
            echo ""
            # WARNING only, not blocking (would be too restrictive)
        fi
    fi
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# NO ISSUES - ALLOW EDIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PostToolUse:Write - Block forbidden imports in code
# Exit 2 to BLOCK, Exit 0 to allow

# Read file content from stdin with timeout
CONTENT=$(timeout 1 cat 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# If empty, allow
[ -z "$CONTENT" ] && exit 0

# Get file path from environment
FILE_PATH="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-}"

# Only check JS/TS files
if ! echo "$FILE_PATH" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then
    exit 0
fi

# Forbidden import patterns
FORBIDDEN=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "from ['\"]xstate"
    "from ['\"]zustand"
    "from ['\"]redux"
    "from ['\"]mobx"
    "from ['\"]next/"
)

for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN[@]}"; do
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
        echo "" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        echo "⛔ BLOCKED: Forbidden import: $pattern" >&2
        echo "File: $FILE_PATH" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        echo "Use instead:" >&2
        echo "  @blueprintjs/* → @patternfly/react-core" >&2
        echo "  zustand/redux/mobx → @belt/tools" >&2
        echo "══════════════════════════════════════════" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PostToolUse:Write - Block forbidden imports in code
# Purpose: Prevent writing code with forbidden package imports
# Exit 2 to BLOCK the action (not just warn)

# Get the file content from stdin
CONTENT=$(cat)

# Get file path from environment (if available)
FILE_PATH="${CLAUDE_FILE_PATH:-unknown}"

# Only check .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx files
if ! echo "$FILE_PATH" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then
    exit 0
fi

# List of forbidden import patterns
FORBIDDEN_IMPORTS=(
    "@blueprintjs"
    "@zazcart"
    "from ['\"]xstate"
    "from ['\"]zustand"
    "from ['\"]redux"
    "from ['\"]mobx"
    "from ['\"]next/"
    "from ['\"]next['\"]"
)

for pattern in "${FORBIDDEN_IMPORTS[@]}"; do
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
        echo ""
        echo "=========================================="
        echo "BLOCKED: Forbidden import detected!"
        echo "=========================================="
        echo ""
        echo "Pattern matched: $pattern"
        echo "File: $FILE_PATH"
        echo ""
        echo "CORRECT IMPORTS:"
        echo "  - @blueprintjs/* → @patternfly/react-core"
        echo "  - zustand/redux/mobx → @belt/tools"
        echo "  - xstate → @belt/tools state machine"
        echo "  - next → use vite instead"
        echo ""
        echo "See CLAUDE.md for required stack."
        echo "=========================================="
        exit 2  # EXIT 2 = BLOCK
    fi
done

# Allow the write to proceed
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PostToolUse:Bash - Unlock after git commit
# Purpose: Remove lock file after successful git commit
# Exit 0 always (post hooks don't block)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

LOCK_FILE=".harness/.lock"
TOOL_INPUT=$(cat)

# Check if this was a git commit command
if echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE "git commit"; then
    # Check if git is now clean
    GIT_STATUS=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)

    if [ -z "$GIT_STATUS" ]; then
        # Git is clean - remove lock if it was for uncommitted changes
        if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
            LOCK_REASON=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)

            if echo "$LOCK_REASON" | grep -q "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES"; then
                # Remove the uncommitted changes line from lock
                grep -v "UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES" "$LOCK_FILE" > "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null
                mv "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null

                # If lock file is now empty, remove it
                if [ ! -s "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
                    rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
                    echo ""
                    echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
                    echo "║ ✅ HARNESS LOCK REMOVED                                       ║"
                    echo "║ Commit successful. You can now proceed with work.             ║"
                    echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
                    echo ""
                fi
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Also check for progress.txt update
if echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE "progress\.txt"; then
    if [ -f ".harness/progress.txt" ] && [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
        # Remove MISSING_PROGRESS from lock
        grep -v "MISSING_PROGRESS" "$LOCK_FILE" > "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" 2>/dev/null
        mv "$LOCK_FILE.tmp" "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null

        if [ ! -s "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
            rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

1→=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T14:42:00Z ===
     2→INTENT: Initialize harness system for MMS project
     3→STATUS: completed
     4→COMPLETED:
     5→  - TASK-000: Created directory structure (.harness/, _memory/, subagents/results/)
     6→  - Created features.json with 27 tasks across 5 phases
     7→  - Created progress.txt (this file)
     8→  - TASK-001: Created hook block-forbidden-packages.sh (blocks npm/bun install of forbidden packages)
     9→  - TASK-002: Created hook block-forbidden-imports.sh (blocks writing code with forbidden imports)
    10→  - Updated CLAUDE.md with SubagentTask pattern (sections 6-9)
    11→  - Updated .claude/settings.json with PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
    12→NEXT:
    13→  - TASK-010 through TASK-040: Execute PHASE 1 parallel extraction (10 subagents)
    14→  - Each subagent saves results to ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
    15→BLOCKERS: None
    16→
    17→=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
    18→Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents
    19→
    20→Key principles:
    21→1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
    22→2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
    23→3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
    24→4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
    25→5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work
    26→
    27→=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
    28→Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
    29→Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
    30→Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
    31→Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
    32→Target: 60fps with 60k tasks
    33→
    34→Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
    35→- Context compression losing constraints
    36→- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
    37→- Executing without permission
    38→- Trusting tests over visual verification
    39→- Using stale training data
    40→
    41→=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
    42→- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
    43→- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
    44→- xstate (over-engineered)
    45→- next (need SPA, not SSR)
    46→- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
    47→- react@18 (must use react@19)
    48→
    49→=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
    50→- react@19.x
    51→- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
    52→- @belt/tools@latest
    53→- @react-three/fiber@9.x
    54→- @react-three/drei@10.x
    55→- three@0.170+
    56→- vite@6.x
    57→
    58→=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:00:00Z ===
    59→INTENT: Enhance hooks to prevent harness violations
    60→STATUS: completed
    61→COMPLETED:
    62→  - Enhanced session-start.sh with lock file system
    63→    - Creates .harness/.lock if uncommitted changes detected
    64→    - Creates .harness/.lock if harness files missing
    65→    - Blocks all edits until issues resolved
    66→  - Created harness-guard.sh (universal blocker)
    67→    - Runs before Bash commands
    68→    - Blocks destructive commands if lock exists
    69→    - Allows git commands to fix the lock
    70→  - Created pre-write-guard.sh
    71→    - Runs before Edit/Write operations
    72→    - Blocks if lock exists
    73→    - Warns if no task registered as in_progress
    74→  - Created post-commit-unlock.sh
    75→    - Runs after Bash commands
    76→    - Removes lock after successful git commit
    77→  - Updated block-forbidden-packages.sh
    78→    - Now also checks for harness lock
    79→    - Allows git and read-only commands when locked
    80→  - Updated settings.json with all hook configurations
    81→
    82→HOOKS NOW ACTIVE:
    83→  SessionStart:
    84→    - session-start.sh → Creates locks if issues detected
    85→  PreToolUse:Bash:
    86→    - block-forbidden-packages.sh → Blocks forbidden + locked
    87→  PreToolUse:Edit:
    88→    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
    89→  PreToolUse:Write:
    90→    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
    91→  PostToolUse:Write:
    92→    - block-forbidden-imports.sh → Blocks forbidden imports
    93→  PostToolUse:Bash:
    94→    - post-commit-unlock.sh → Removes lock after commit
    95→
    96→LOCK FILE SYSTEM:
    97→  - .harness/.lock created at session start if:
    98→    1. Git has uncommitted changes (UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES)
    99→    2. .harness/progress.txt missing (MISSING_PROGRESS)
   100→    3. .harness/features.json missing (MISSING_FEATURES)
   101→  - Lock BLOCKS all destructive operations
   102→  - Lock removed after: git commit (if git clean)
   103→  - Allowed when locked: git status/add/commit, read-only commands
   104→
   105→NEXT: ✅ Completed in previous session
   106→
   107→=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:10:00Z ===
   108→INTENT: Execute PHASE 1 - Parallel Knowledge Extraction (10 subagents)
   109→STATUS: completed

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T14:42:00Z ===
INTENT: Initialize harness system for MMS project
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - TASK-000: Created directory structure (.harness/, _memory/, subagents/results/)
  - Created features.json with 27 tasks across 5 phases
  - Created progress.txt (this file)
  - TASK-001: Created hook block-forbidden-packages.sh (blocks npm/bun install of forbidden packages)
  - TASK-002: Created hook block-forbidden-imports.sh (blocks writing code with forbidden imports)
  - Updated CLAUDE.md with SubagentTask pattern (sections 6-9)
  - Updated .claude/settings.json with PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
NEXT:
  - TASK-010 through TASK-040: Execute PHASE 1 parallel extraction (10 subagents)
  - Each subagent saves results to ./subagents/results/20260106-TASK-XXX.jsonl
BLOCKERS: None

=== HARNESS REFERENCE ===
Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Key principles:
1. Each session starts with NO memory - read progress.txt first
2. Features tracked in features.json (passes: true/false)
3. ONE feature per session, visual verify, commit
4. Subagents save results to ./subagents/results/{YYYYMMDD}-{TASK-ID}.jsonl
5. Always register intent BEFORE executing work

=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===
Project: MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)
Client: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
Problem: Schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains across 12 years
Solution: Semantic Zoom canvas (heatmap -> DNA blocks -> task cards)
Target: 60fps with 60k tasks

Previous attempts: 5+ failed due to:
- Context compression losing constraints
- AI hallucinating wrong packages (blueprintjs instead of patternfly)
- Executing without permission
- Trusting tests over visual verification
- Using stale training data

=== FORBIDDEN PACKAGES ===
- @blueprintjs/* (use @patternfly/react-core)
- @zazcart/* (doesn't exist)
- xstate (over-engineered)
- next (need SPA, not SSR)
- zustand/redux/mobx (use @belt/tools)
- react@18 (must use react@19)

=== REQUIRED PACKAGES ===
- react@19.x
- @patternfly/react-core@6.x
- @belt/tools@latest
- @react-three/fiber@9.x
- @react-three/drei@10.x
- three@0.170+
- vite@6.x

=== SESSION: 2026-01-06T15:00:00Z ===
INTENT: Enhance hooks to prevent harness violations
STATUS: completed
COMPLETED:
  - Enhanced session-start.sh with lock file system
    - Creates .harness/.lock if uncommitted changes detected
    - Creates .harness/.lock if harness files missing
    - Blocks all edits until issues resolved
  - Created harness-guard.sh (universal blocker)
    - Runs before Bash commands
    - Blocks destructive commands if lock exists
    - Allows git commands to fix the lock
  - Created pre-write-guard.sh
    - Runs before Edit/Write operations
    - Blocks if lock exists
    - Warns if no task registered as in_progress
  - Created post-commit-unlock.sh
    - Runs after Bash commands
    - Removes lock after successful git commit
  - Updated block-forbidden-packages.sh
    - Now also checks for harness lock
    - Allows git and read-only commands when locked
  - Updated settings.json with all hook configurations

HOOKS NOW ACTIVE:
  SessionStart:
    - session-start.sh → Creates locks if issues detected
  PreToolUse:Bash:
    - block-forbidden-packages.sh → Blocks forbidden + locked
  PreToolUse:Edit:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PreToolUse:Write:
    - pre-write-guard.sh → Blocks if locked
  PostToolUse:Write:
    - block-forbidden-imports.sh → Blocks forbidden imports
  PostToolUse:Bash:
    - post-commit-unlock.sh → Removes lock after commit

LOCK FILE SYSTEM:
  - .harness/.lock created at session start if:
    1. Git has uncommitted changes (UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES)
    2. .harness/progress.txt missing (MISSING_PROGRESS)
    3. .harness/features.json missing (MISSING_FEATURES)
  - Lock BLOCKS all destructive operations
  - Lock removed after: git commit (if git clean)
  - Allowed when locked: git status/add/commit, read-only commands

NEXT: ✅ Completed in previous session

lines:
-      "prompt": "# SUBAGENT TASK: SPEC-003

## CONTEXT
Você tem:
- SPEC-001: PatternFly 6 components research
- SPEC-002: DESIGN_SPEC concepts adapted to PatternFly

## OBJECTIVE
Criar um documento de especificação EXECUTÁVEL que equipes UX, Dev e Produto possam usar.

## INPUT FILES
- ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-001.jsonl
- ./subagents/results/20260106-SPEC-002.jsonl
- ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/RFQ.md (requisitos do cliente)
- ~/antonio/mms/deck/docs/REQUIREMENTS.md (requisitos mapeados)

## OUTPUT STRUCTURE
Criar: ./_memory/MMS_EXECUTABLE_SPEC.md

Seções:
1. RESUMO EXECUTIVO (1 página)
   - O que é o MMS
   - Stack técnico
   - Features principais

2. PARA EQUIPE DE PRODUTO
   - User stories mapeadas do RFQ
   - Prioridades (Sandbox Mode é #1)
   - Critérios de aceitação

3. PARA EQUIPE DE UX
   - Layout com PatternFly (Page/Masthead/Sidebar)
   - Componentes a usar (PatternFly names)
   - Semantic zoom behavior
   - NÃO incluir cores customizadas (usar PatternFly tokens)

4. PARA EQUIPE DE DEV
   - Tech stack exato (React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9, etc.)
   - File structure proposta
   - Componentes a implementar
   - Performance targets

5. PARA QA
   - Visual verification checklist
   - Performance criteria
   - Casos de teste prioritários

## RESTRICTIONS
- NÃO mencionar BlueprintJS
- USAR PatternFly 6 para UI
- USAR design tokens do PatternFly (não cores customizadas)
- Ser CONCISO e ACIONÁVEL",
